description.default: Cracked asphalt and broken promises. The kind of lot that absorbs what happens to it without keeping records.

time.morning: Dawn hasn't done the place any favours. Grease stains and cigarette butts pass for the morning crowd.
time.afternoon: Heat shimmers off the blacktop. Every car baking in the sun carries the marks of the city it came from.
time.evening: The lot empties like a bar at last call — fast, and without a backward glance.
time.night: One lamp still burns. The rest are dead, same as every promise this lot ever made.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Every plate, every dent, every smear of mud carries the record of where that car has been.
attribute.AGILITY: The diagonal between the rows cuts the transit time in half. Most people don't take it.

gender.male: A man who moves through here like he owns it doesn't get a second look. The lot decides that in about four steps.
gender.female: The exits in a place like this sit at the edges of sight — there, and findable, for anyone who thinks to look.

state.good: Fresh asphalt lines the lot in crisp white paint. The surface is even and uncracked, recently sealed against the weather.
state.normal: Weathered asphalt shows the marks of regular use. Most lines are still readable, though several have faded to grey.
state.bad: Buckled asphalt heaves through broken lane markings. Weeds push up through wide fissures in the tarmac, and puddles pool in the sunken patches.

description.default: A hundred cars and not one of them parked here by accident. The lot is big enough to swallow an afternoon and small enough that nothing quite disappears.

time.morning: Workers funnel in before the sun gets mean. During shift changes, the foot traffic moves in one direction and nobody's counting heads.
time.afternoon: The open forecourt offers nothing to stand behind. Still figures draw attention.
time.evening: The tide goes out. Engines turn over in the dark. A few hesitate — catching, faltering, trying again.
time.night: The lot sprawls under a bruised sky. Every shadow that doesn't belong to a car or a lamp post belongs to something else.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Three vehicles here don't fit the zip code. Plates, models, parking position — each one a small argument against coincidence.
attribute.STEALTH: A space this size offers a hundred places to disappear. The best three sit near the perimeter — cover, sightlines, and an exit within four steps.
attribute.AGILITY: The rows between cars offer clear lanes and blind angles in equal measure. No straight lines. No witnesses at the right distance.

gender.male: A man who strolls a lot like he's headed nowhere reads as harmless. The better the tail, the more unremarkable the walk.
gender.female: A woman in a lot this size draws a different kind of attention. A few faces clock the entrance. What they see is up for interpretation.

state.good: Wide lanes stretch across freshly sealed asphalt. The painted directional arrows are crisp, and the perimeter curbs are freshly edged.
state.normal: A few surface cracks run through the asphalt and the lane markings have started to thin. The lot is functional but shows its years.
state.bad: The asphalt has fractured into rough sections separated by deep cracks. Only fragments of the original lane markings remain, and rusted signage leans at odd angles.

description.default: Concrete and shadow. The kind of place where things get settled without paperwork.

time.morning: Early arrivals echo off the ceiling. Engines and footsteps bouncing off concrete in the half-light.
time.afternoon: Every level occupied. Every car a question. The answers, if there are any, are parked somewhere in this concrete dark.
time.evening: The stream of departures fills the levels with movement and noise. Cover comes free at this hour.
time.night: The garage breathes on its own — creaks, drips, the groan of settling steel. The kind of quiet that isn't quiet at all.

attribute.STEALTH: Pillars every twelve feet. Each one a hard shadow and a reason to stop. The geometry of this place rewards patience.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Sound bounces off concrete in ways most people never bother to learn. Every footstep has a direction. Every engine note has a floor.
attribute.AGILITY: Every ramp, every turn, every blind corner maps to the next. A structure like this reads itself in about ten steps.

gender.male: A man who stays in the shadows of a place like this becomes part of its architecture. The dark absorbs him. Nobody accounts for shadows.
gender.female: The stairwell lights are noted by anyone who uses them. A woman who moves through a garage without hesitation changes the calculation — working with the space, not against it.

state.good: Clean concrete walls reflect bright overhead lighting. The painted floor markings are sharp, the stairwell handrails freshly coated, and the drainage channels clear.
state.normal: The concrete shows the typical grey staining of a working garage. Overhead lights flicker in one section; most floor markings are still legible.
state.bad: Concrete spalls from the ceiling in sections, exposing rusted rebar beneath. Water stains stripe the walls in dark vertical lines, and half the overhead fixtures are dark.

description.default: Six levels of concrete and secrets. The kind of structure that accumulates what people carry in and don't carry out.

time.morning: The morning shift spirals upward in a slow parade of commuters who don't know what walks among them.
time.afternoon: Activity on every level. The noise and movement distribute attention across six floors. Cover comes standard.
time.evening: Rush hour bleeds the garage dry. By the time the last engine fades, the structure belongs to whoever stayed.
time.night: The exit signs throw red light on empty concrete. Five ways out of a structure this size, and most people only know one.

attribute.STEALTH: A structure this size has more blind spots than a crooked precinct. Camera angles overlap in theory. In practice, the gaps are generous.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The cameras are visible on the way in. Half of them look older than the building itself — housings cracked, lenses fogged.
attribute.AGILITY: The stairs run along the exterior wall. Fast, predictable, open at both ends. The elevators are a single point of exposure.

gender.male: A man who moves through a structure like he built it draws no attention. Sure, deliberate, clearly with a destination — nobody's problem.
gender.female: A woman moving through a multi-storey alone gets assessed at every level. She reads that assessment. Uses it.

state.good: Six levels of clean, well-lit concrete. Each ramp is clearly marked and the structural columns show no sign of deterioration.
state.normal: Grease and tyre rubber have given the concrete floor a permanent grey sheen. The overhead lighting is adequate but uneven, and a few column bases show surface cracking.
state.bad: Exposed aggregate and scaling concrete mark every wall. Long streaks of rust bleed from ceiling joints, and the drainage trenches are clogged with decades of debris.

description.default: Fluorescent light and stale coffee. The kind of place where lives quietly fall apart between the shelves.

time.morning: First customers of the day. The cashier looks like the night never ended. For them, maybe it didn't.
time.afternoon: The slow afternoon burns. Bored staff and restless regulars. One person in here is waiting on something specific. The others are just burning time.
time.evening: After-shift crowd. Tired, distracted, and telling the truth without knowing it.
time.night: Three a.m. and the place is lit up like a crime scene. One customer too many for this hour.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Security mirror angles, till placement, the back-room door left two inches open. The room arranges itself into a story for anyone reading it.
attribute.INTUITION: The man by the cooler has been staring at the same six-pack for four minutes. That's not indecision. That's something else.

gender.male: A man who buys a coffee with cash and leaves is nothing to the cashier. Unmemorable by design. The transaction doesn't stick.
gender.female: A woman who browses slowly and says little creates a silence the cashier will fill. People always do. The information comes out on its own.

state.good: The shopfront glass is spotless and the signage backlit and even. The exterior paint is fresh and the entrance canopy sits level without rust.
state.normal: The signage has one dead bulb and the exterior paint shows small chips near the door frame. The glass is clean but carries the thin haze of old streaks.
state.bad: The exterior paint is blistered and peeling from the corners outward. Several letters in the sign are dark, and the entrance canopy sags on a bent bracket.

description.default: Pump logs, forecourt cameras, and a shop stocked for people who didn't plan ahead. A fuel station with a convenience store records everything — card details, timestamps, the face at the counter. Most people filling up don't think about that.

time.morning: Early fill-ups before the commute locks in. Drivers on automatic, buying fuel and coffee without looking at anything too closely. Wide-open observation window.
time.afternoon: The pumps quiet down and the shop carries the trade. People stopping for reasons that have nothing to do with fuel. The gap between cover story and real intent shows in the time they spend deciding what to buy.
time.evening: Rush-hour volume. Every pump occupied and the shop a revolving door. Busy enough to move through without standing out. Not so busy the wrong detail gets lost.
time.night: Forecourt lights hard against the dark and nothing on the road. One attendant, one angle of coverage, and a fill-up that gets remembered because there's nothing else to remember.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Camera positions, pump layout, the one approach angle that doesn't feed the monitor behind the counter. The forecourt reads like a diagram for anyone who bothers to map it.
attribute.AGILITY: Fuel, one item from the shop, and the forecourt clears fast. The transaction is logged. The exit route isn't.
attribute.INTUITION: The car at pump six filled up ten minutes ago and hasn't moved. Drivers who've paid don't wait at the pump. Someone's watching something.

gender.male: Cash at the pump, nothing from the shop. No record past the nozzle sensor. A stop that leaves almost no trace.
gender.female: The attendant clocks the stop — standard procedure. What they log is the transaction. What was noted on the way in doesn't make it into the record.

state.good: The canopy overhead is clean white with crisp brand colours. The pump islands are freshly painted and the forecourt surface shows no staining.
state.normal: Fuel stains mark the forecourt around the pump islands. The canopy paint is intact but dull, and the price display boards show wear on their edges.
state.bad: The canopy has rusted along its fascia panels, and sheets of its surface finish have peeled away. The forecourt concrete is cracked and deeply stained, and the pump housing covers are yellowed and split.

description.default: Grease and noise and the steady misery of people who stopped expecting anything better. The city's honest face, stripped of pretence.

time.morning: The breakfast queue snakes to the door. Everyone's hungover or hasn't slept. Either way, their guard is down.
time.afternoon: Packed at noon. A thousand conversations happening at once. The right one surfaces for anyone who knows which register to tune to.
time.evening: Families and teenagers occupy the booths, all of them watching their screens, none of them watching each other.
time.night: Night shift crew, hollow-eyed and running on spite. The real city, after-hours, laid bare.

attribute.CHARISMA: The counter staff talks to people who seem approachable. The right face, the right tone, and the server fills the silence with more than the menu ever would.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Twelve tables, forty faces, and one person eating alone who arrived before the rush and hasn't touched their food.
attribute.STAMINA: The grease and the noise accumulate. Most people stop paying attention to anything but the table in front of them inside twenty minutes. The room stays indifferent to that.

gender.male: The corner booth, back to the wall, gives a clear view of the room and the door. A man who takes it looks like he belongs here. The booth doesn't announce itself — it just works.
gender.female: A woman who sits where she can see the door draws a neighbour who wants to make conversation. She lets him think he succeeded. The information flows in one direction.

state.good: Bold signage stands crisp above a clean facade. The drive-through lane markings are fresh and the window glazing is clear and unscratched.
state.normal: The facade is intact but the bright colour panels have mellowed with exposure. Drive-through lane markings are still visible but worn at the corners.
state.bad: Cracked cladding panels have pulled away from the facade in sections. The signage is partially dark, and the drive-through lane markings are barely traceable on the faded asphalt.

description.default: Seven-dollar coffee and secrets sold by the pound. Everyone in here is running from something or toward something worse.

time.morning: The morning rush brings them in loud and caffeinated. Loose mouths, tight schedules — money can't buy discretion.
time.afternoon: The afternoon crowd settles in with laptops and long drinks. They think they're hiding. They're not.
time.evening: Quieter now. The ones who remain have nowhere better to be. The attrition of the evening hour leaves only the committed.
time.night: Last customers of the night nursing cold cups. The barista wants to close. The want is written on his face. The customers don't read it.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: A corner table, full view of the room, one coffee, and ninety minutes of plausible presence. The information accumulates in layers — screens, faces, half-heard conversations.
attribute.MEMORY: The regular at the end of the bar carries a familiar face — the kind that belongs to a different context, a different problem. The weight of it doesn't diminish with distance.
attribute.CHARISMA: The barista starts the order before the customer reaches the counter. A regular. The easiest cover a coffee shop offers — familiar, expected, part of the furniture.

gender.male: A man with a newspaper at a coffee shop table doesn't register. The newspaper doesn't even have to be current. The posture is enough.
gender.female: Two men near the window have been watching the same woman since she sat down. Their faces are patient. So is hers.

state.good: A clean rendered exterior with matching window trim and an unscratched sandwich board stand. The painted shopfront frames are uniform and crisp.
state.normal: The exterior render shows hairline cracks near the window joints. The window trim paint has begun to chalk at its leading edges.
state.bad: Damp has lifted sections of exterior render from the wall beneath. The window frames are raw in places where the paint has fully peeled, and the entrance step has chipped away at both corners.

description.default: Everything for sale, including information — though most shopkeepers don't know they're selling it.

time.morning: Freshly stocked shelves and a shopkeeper who hasn't had their guard up long enough to keep it steady.
time.afternoon: The slow burn of a quiet afternoon. The owner has time to read the room. So does anyone who walks into it.
time.evening: Closing time creeps in. People in a hurry say more than they mean to.
time.night: Shuttered. The grate is down. Whatever the shop knew, it stopped sharing six hours ago.

attribute.CHARISMA: An owner who greets customers like old money has made a calculation. The customer who carries themselves accordingly gets the best service in the shop.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Mismatched inventory tags. A back shelf accessed too recently. Small things. None of them accidental.
attribute.STEALTH: The aisles are narrow and the sight lines are broken by shelving. Nobody in here is being paid to watch carefully. The browsing customer is barely registered.

gender.male: A man who asks a dumb question about a product he doesn't need will get five minutes of the owner's time. One answer in those five minutes will be worth having.
gender.female: The shopkeeper relaxes the moment a woman walks in. Threat assessment: none. The ease is visible in the posture, the tone, the detail freely given.

state.good: A freshly painted shopfront with clean glazing and an intact awning. The entrance threshold is uncracked and the door hardware polished.
state.normal: The exterior paint has begun to chalk and one awning panel is slightly faded compared to the others. The glazing carries faint wiper marks.
state.bad: The awning frame has bent in one section, pulling the fabric out of alignment. Paint has flaked from the door frame down to bare wood, and the glass carries a permanent film.

description.default: Prescriptions and secrets. People are never more honest than when they're sick and scared.

time.morning: Early customers collecting scripts they needed yesterday. Desperation has a particular walk.
time.afternoon: The lunchtime queue stretches down the aisle. A pharmacist under this kind of pressure makes small mistakes.
time.evening: Last calls before closing. The ones who come late don't want to be seen coming at all.
time.night: Dark and locked. Only the emergency window glows. Whatever brought someone out this late matters.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The shelves carry their own record — low stock where demand is high, over-ordering where something's being managed. Out-of-place product in the wrong section. The inventory tells a story.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every prescription tells a story. The chapters people carry out in paper bags are the ones they didn't come here to share.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The pharmacist's hands froze for half a second on one name. Just half a second. Enough.

gender.male: A man who lingers by the vitamins long enough catches the counter conversation without being part of it. People say more than they intend to in places like this.
gender.female: The pharmacist knows every customer by prescription history and by name. The relationship between a pharmacist and a regular customer is built on trust and repetition.

state.good: A clean clinical exterior with even signage lighting and unscratched window glazing. The entrance is wide, level, and recently resurfaced.
state.normal: The signage lighting is slightly uneven and one entrance mat sits raised at a corner. The exterior cladding is clean but shows minor surface crazing.
state.bad: Delaminated cladding panels have come away at the corners of the building. The sign box has darkened in sections, and the entrance canopy has developed a pronounced lean.

description.default: Money is just a way of keeping score. This branch keeps the tally. Someone in it has been adjusting the numbers.

time.morning: Doors just open. Nervous customers and tellers who haven't been lied to yet today. Give it time.
time.afternoon: Lunch-hour queue, harried staff, and the midday cover of noise. The branch does its dirtiest business in plain sight.
time.evening: Counting down the drawers. That's when the numbers stop lying and the people start.
time.night: Locked. The ATM hums in the dark. Someone's using it at this hour for a reason.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The floor plan reads itself in under ten seconds — vault access, teller positions, the one camera with the bad angle on the far wall.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: The guard straightens when someone walks past without slowing. Authority reads in posture. The response is automatic.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The customer three places ahead in the queue is sweating through a suit jacket on a cool day. That's not the weather.

gender.male: A man who carries himself like he has an appointment gets treated as though he does. The assumption is automatic. The branch extends the courtesy before the question is asked.
gender.female: The branch manager approaches before a number is taken. A woman who walks in like she owns the room gets seen first. The branch manager makes that calculation without being asked.

state.good: Polished granite cladding and clean brass trim give the building a solid, well-maintained face. The entrance door handles are free of tarnish.
state.normal: The granite cladding has surface scratches at shoulder height and the brass trim has dulled. The entry canopy is structurally sound but shows weathering along its seams.
state.bad: Sections of granite cladding have been repaired with mismatched mortar. The brass trim is green with oxidation, and one ground-floor window has been boarded in plywood.

description.default: Everyone comes here with something to clean. Not all of it fits in a drum.

time.morning: The early crowd does their laundry before work. What they're washing out, that's the question.
time.afternoon: The machines drone on. Afternoon light through dirty glass. A good place to sit and think — the sound covers the quiet, and the quiet covers the thinking.
time.evening: After-work crowd. Tired, automatic, not watching what's around them. The room is full of inward attention.
time.night: Two people and the sound of spinning cycles. At this hour, people are either working unusual shifts or they're hiding something. Sometimes both.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The man in the corner hasn't looked at his laundry once. He's watching the door.
attribute.MEMORY: That face belongs to a different neighbourhood. Different context, same trouble — the kind that follows a person from postcode to postcode.
attribute.STEALTH: A figure folding laundry draws no attention. The laundromat is built for occupied waiting. Everyone here is absorbed in their own cycle.

gender.male: A man with a magazine he isn't reading blends into the wait. The laundromat absorbs him alongside the other slow hours and the spinning drums.
gender.female: A woman chatting with the one next to her is unremarkable in a laundromat. The neighbour, the landlord, the troubles — the information moves freely in a space where time is being killed.

state.good: Clean white render and clear shopfront windows reveal the row of machines inside. The exterior signage is fully illuminated and the drainage gutter is clear.
state.normal: Soap residue has left pale streaks below the ventilation grille. The render is sound but carries scattered patches of old gum near the entrance.
state.bad: Moisture has swollen the door frame out of true. The render has bubbled and cracked near the drainage pipe, and the ventilation grille is choked with lint.

description.default: The most honest conversations in any city happen in a hair salon. People say everything when someone's working on their head.

time.morning: First appointments of the day. The stylists are fresh and the clients are loose-lipped.
time.afternoon: Full house, every chair occupied, and the noise level a cover for anything said in it. The conversations layer over one another. The room processes information without knowing it.
time.evening: Last cuts of the day. The relaxed end of a long shift is when the real talk happens.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever the chairs knew, they gave it up six hours ago.

attribute.CHARISMA: The social rhythm of a hair salon has a tempo — regulars, callbacks, the easy banter of familiarity. Someone who matches it reads as a known face, regardless of whether they are.
attribute.EMPATHY: The chatter sounds like small talk. It isn't. The spaces between the words carry as much as the words themselves — the pauses, the redirections, the laugh that comes a beat too late.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Three conversations happening simultaneously. The room generates information at a rate most people can't process. It all goes into the air.

gender.male: The only man in the room is known to be there by everyone in it. The attention it generates is brief and bright. Useful while it holds.
gender.female: The mirror in a salon shows every face in the room at once. A woman who reads it properly is watching an audience that doesn't know it's on stage.

state.good: A neat shopfront with a freshly painted door and clear, clean glass. The awning is taut and its colour is even across the full width.
state.normal: The awning has faded slightly on the sun-facing side. The exterior paintwork is intact but shows minor crazing near the window reveal.
state.bad: The awning frame has corroded and one end hangs free of its mounting. The window frame paint has blistered and the door threshold is cracked across its width.

description.default: Iron and sweat and the quiet desperation of people trying to outrun something. The place doesn't judge. It just keeps count.

time.morning: The early crowd punishes themselves before dawn. Either very disciplined or very guilty.
time.afternoon: Midday regulars. Less competition, better sight lines. The room holds its breath between sets.
time.evening: After-work rush. Every frustration in the city works itself out on a machine. Some frustrations don't belong in a gym.
time.night: The diehards. The ones who can't sleep or won't. The reasons are written on their faces.

attribute.STRENGTH: The heaviest rack sits in the corner, untouched by most. The iron doesn't negotiate. The space is built for things that weigh more than a bad day.
attribute.STAMINA: The cardio floor runs the length of the building. Rows of machines, all of them counting. The distance this place demands shows in the faces of those who've been here since before the light changed.
attribute.AGILITY: The training floor runs in a natural circuit — free weights along the far wall, machines flanking the centre, mats at the back. Every corner visible from the entrance. No dead ends.

gender.male: Two regulars nod at newcomers and hold equipment for people they've never met. Some spaces run on old, unwritten codes of conduct.
gender.female: A woman works the free weights in the far corner. The stares come and go. The weights don't care, and neither does she.

state.good: A robust brick or render facade with clean glazed panels and fresh signage. The entrance mat is new and the door hardware is tight.
state.normal: The facade is intact but the lower panels show scuff marks from equipment deliveries. The signage is readable but the illumination is uneven.
state.bad: The facade panels have been patched in places with sealant that has since yellowed. Several sign letters have fallen, and the entrance canopy is rust-stained along its underside.

description.default: Sick people, tired doctors, and a filing system that knows everything about everyone. The files are the point.

time.morning: Waiting room filling up before nine. People who came early are either very ill or very motivated. The difference reads in the posture.
time.afternoon: Long afternoon queue, overworked staff, the kind of exhaustion that makes people careless with information.
time.evening: Last appointments running late. The clinic wants to close. Two more minutes is all anyone in the building has left.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever the building holds after hours requires a different approach.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The prescription pad on the counter. The dosages on the display shelf. Everything in this room offers more than the receptionist would.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every patient in the waiting room is scared of something. The ones scared of the wrong thing are easy to spot — the eyes go to the door, not the clock.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The desk has been rearranged recently — clipboard repositioned, prescription pad covered. The room adapts when it senses it is being read.

gender.male: The reception desk is the first checkpoint and the last line of courtesy. The staff are trained to manage the gap between what people say is wrong and what is actually wrong.
gender.female: Through the door and past the reception line — a woman moving with purpose in a clinic reads as staff. The question never gets asked.

state.good: A clean rendered building with even paintwork and unscratched entrance glazing. The accessible ramp is freshly marked and the handrails are solid.
state.normal: The exterior paintwork shows hairline cracking near the window reveals. The accessible ramp is intact but the tactile paving at the base has two missing sections.
state.bad: The render has bubbled and separated from the wall in patches. The entrance canopy bracket has corroded and the glazed entrance screen carries a spider crack across one pane.

description.default: People say things in a dental chair they'd never say anywhere else. Pain is the world's oldest truth serum.

time.morning: First appointments wear the face of people who've been dreading this since yesterday. The waiting room runs on a low, familiar dread.
time.afternoon: The books have fallen behind. Waiting room patience has worn thin. The room has the texture of accumulated frustration.
time.evening: The last patient shuffles out with a numb jaw and no filter. Catches the door on the way out. Says something true.
time.night: Empty office, chairs tilted back in the dark. The room holds its breath without anyone in it.

attribute.EMPATHY: The cost of sitting in that chair is written on every face in the waiting room. The shared dread is the common ground.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The x-rays on the lightbox are interesting for reasons the dentist wouldn't appreciate.
attribute.PERCEPTION: There's a tension between the dentist and the hygienist that has nothing to do with a billing dispute.

gender.male: A man in the waiting room chair nearest the corridor, turning the pages of a magazine no one has opened since last year. The picture of ordinary.
gender.female: The receptionist engages with the kind of courtesy that doesn't come with a clipboard. The conversation moves. Some environments still run that way.

state.good: A neat shopfront or suite-level facade with clean glass and precise signage. The entrance threshold is level and the intercom panel is new.
state.normal: The exterior paint has begun to chalk at the top edge of the facade panel. The signage is legible but one illuminated letter is slightly dimmer than the rest.
state.bad: Paint has peeled from the facade panel down to bare primer in one section. The signage illumination has failed entirely, and the intercom panel casing is cracked and discoloured.

description.default: Animals don't lie, and the people who love them are halfway honest. A good place to ask questions.

time.morning: First clients of the day — worried owners, anxious animals, and no reason yet to be on guard.
time.afternoon: Routine visits and the kind of chatter that happens when everyone's been waiting too long.
time.evening: Last few cases. The day's weight shows on the staff. Weight makes people talk.
time.night: Emergency hours only. Whatever brings someone here at this time of night — it matters.

attribute.EMPATHY: The clinic cat makes its rounds without hurry. Animals don't read threat where there isn't one. The waiting room takes its cue.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The conversation in exam room two involves a word that doesn't belong in a routine check-up.
attribute.INTUITION: The owner waiting in the corner hasn't touched their phone in forty minutes. They're thinking. Hard.

gender.male: A man holds the door. A client takes it as an opening and gives a full account of their last three visits in return. Politeness is a currency that pays.
gender.female: The vet tech speaks across the counter without the usual professional distance. Four minutes of conversation, the kind that takes others an hour.

state.good: A clean-rendered single-storey building with intact signage and unscratched glazing. The entrance path is clear and the exterior lighting is fully functional.
state.normal: The rendered exterior shows minor crazing and one external light fitting has a cracked housing. The signage lettering is intact but slightly faded.
state.bad: The render has spalled away near the base of the building. The exterior lighting is reduced to one functioning fixture, and the signage box is stained with a long rust streak from a failing fixing.

description.default: Small humans, big noise, and staff trained to notice anything out of the ordinary. Any adult without a child in tow is, by definition, out of the ordinary.

time.morning: Drop-off hour. Parents distracted, kids crying, staff stretched. The easiest time to enter unscrutinised.
time.afternoon: Activities in session. The organised chaos is a kind of cover.
time.evening: Pick-up time. Parents running late say things they wouldn't say in a calmer moment.
time.night: Locked and dark. Any light here after hours is exactly as suspicious as it looks.

attribute.EMPATHY: A child at the corner table has been watching the door. Not playing. Watching. There is more in that small face than anyone has thought to ask about.
attribute.CHARISMA: The staff read strangers as harmless or not within seconds. People who work with children develop that instinct fast. It is a calibrated and reliable instrument.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One child has been watching the door. Not waiting for a parent. Watching for something else.

gender.male: An unaccompanied adult male in a daycare centre attracts the full protocol. The sign-in sheet, the escort, the visible watchfulness — it's by design.
gender.female: A woman is waved through the entrance with a smile. The assumption holds long enough to matter. The window is narrow.

state.good: Bright painted render and clearly glazed windows behind a well-maintained perimeter fence. The entrance gate and intercom post are clean and fully upright.
state.normal: The exterior paint shows crazing and minor chips near the fence line. The intercom post has a shallow dent and the perimeter fence is straight but weathered.
state.bad: The exterior paint has peeled extensively from the fence-side wall. The perimeter fence has bent sections, and the intercom post has been knocked out of plumb.

description.default: Crayon drawings and the kind of secrets kids carry without knowing they're carrying them. The whole place is built on good faith — which makes it fragile.

time.morning: Assembly echoes down the corridor. Every adult in the building is occupied. The best time to move unseen.
time.afternoon: Classes in session. The halls are empty between periods — a window of maybe forty seconds.
time.evening: After-school activities. Fewer staff, looser protocols, kids who've been here long enough to drop their guard.
time.night: Locked and dark. A janitor's cart outside one room. The building keeps its own hours.

attribute.EMPATHY: Kids read sincerity faster than any polygraph. Genuine interest is legible to them in ways adults have forgotten.
attribute.CHARISMA: Teachers move naturally around a certain kind of presence — the kind that carries the quiet authority of someone who belongs here.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The corner of one classroom breaks the pattern. The usual visual noise of a primary school — and then one thing that doesn't belong.

gender.male: An adult male visitor follows the full protocol — sign-in, lanyard, escort. The procedure is deliberate. It slows things down. That is the point.
gender.female: A woman moves through the office with the easy manner of a visiting professional. Two teachers assume she's the new reading coordinator. The assumption goes uncorrected.

state.good: A clean brick or render building with intact windows and freshly painted trim. The main entrance canopy is level and the surrounding grounds are neat.
state.normal: The building exterior shows general weathering. Window frames need repainting in sections, and the entrance canopy has a minor stain from a blocked gutter joint.
state.bad: Several window frames have rotted at the corners and been repaired with sealant. The entrance canopy has a visible sag on one side, and the exterior brickwork is stained with persistent damp below two windows.

description.default: Hierarchy, tension, and information passed in corridors at speeds most adults can't follow. The social architecture is visible to anyone who knows how to read it.

time.morning: Social geometry re-establishing itself for another day. The first thirty seconds of the morning reveals the whole structure.
time.afternoon: Between-period surge. Forty seconds of controlled chaos in every corridor. A brief window of maximum cover.
time.evening: Practice, rehearsals, detention. The building runs multiple operations after the bell. Not all of them are on the official schedule.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever happened here today left its mark on tomorrow.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The social map of this building is legible — faster to read, perhaps, from the outside looking in than from within it.
attribute.CHARISMA: Students and staff both respond to a certain kind of presence. The kind that reads as official to anyone not looking closely.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The student who should be in class isn't. Three possible reasons. One of them connects to something larger than a skipped period.

gender.male: An adult male gets assessed immediately — visitor, authority, or threat. The calculation happens in seconds. Authority reads cleanest.
gender.female: A woman at the front office moves through easily. A student's name mentioned in the right tone opens doors that formal channels wouldn't.

state.good: A substantial school building with clean masonry and intact window glazing. The main entrance steps are even and the railings are freshly painted.
state.normal: The masonry shows the expected weathering of a working school. Paint has worn from the entrance railings in patches, and one set of steps has a hairline crack across the riser.
state.bad: Sections of the masonry have been tuck-pointed with mismatched mortar. The entrance railings are rust-pitted and loose at their base fixings, and several upper-floor windows have cracked panes filled with tape.

description.default: Second chances and the quiet ambition of people who got a late start. Some of them are running from something. It shows in the way they carry themselves.

time.morning: Morning lectures. Students who chose early slots are either very motivated or very employed. Worth knowing which.
time.afternoon: Study groups, common areas, the social glue of people who commute in and commute out. Good for a conversation.
time.evening: Evening classes. Older students, working adults, people fitting education around other lives. Less polished, more honest.
time.night: Campus quiet. Library still lit. Late students and the kind of silence that holds things.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The notice board tells more than the admin office would. Who's recruiting, who's advertising, who's watching.
attribute.MEMORY: Academic environments run on paperwork. Every sheet passed over a desk, every form pinned to a corkboard — a trail for anyone who remembers what they've seen.
attribute.CHARISMA: Faculty and students both engage with the right kind of presence. Genuine curiosity is rare enough that people respond when they encounter it.

gender.male: A male presence of a certain bearing gets taken for faculty without question. The assumption extends access.
gender.female: Students approach a woman with questions, assuming she's enrolled. The conversation opens both ways.

state.good: A campus of clean rendered or brick buildings with clear signage and intact window glazing. The paved plazas between buildings are level and well-drained.
state.normal: The building facades show surface weathering and some window frames need attention at their sills. Plaza paving has settled in sections, creating shallow ridges.
state.bad: Render has delaminated from the facade of the main block in large sections. Several windows have been replaced with mismatched glazing, and the plaza paving is cracked and weedy.

description.default: These people run toward the thing everyone else runs from. The culture inside is built on that inversion — controlled, physical, loyal.

time.morning: Morning check is ritual — gear, vehicles, the discipline of people who know the cost of being unprepared.
time.afternoon: A call-out empties the station in forty seconds. The building goes from full to hollow in the time it takes to register the alarm.
time.evening: Evening shift settling in. The shared meal is where the real conversation happens.
time.night: Between calls, the station breathes. The overnight hours carry a particular quality of stillness.

attribute.STRENGTH: The crew reads a visitor in one look. Physical presence either passes that test or it doesn't. There is no appeal.
attribute.STAMINA: Physical endurance is the baseline currency of this place. The crew's attention goes to those who carry it without advertising it.
attribute.AGILITY: The station's interior is an obstacle course of equipment, hoses, and hardware. Old training reads in the body — the way someone moves through a space without thinking about it.

gender.male: The crew reads a man by his hands and his posture. The decision comes fast. It usually lands correctly.
gender.female: The station goes quiet when a woman walks in — not hostile, just recalibrating. The pause is brief. Work follows.

state.good: A solid brick or rendered building with clean apparatus bay doors and freshly painted bay aprons. The exterior brickwork is pointed and the windows intact.
state.normal: The apparatus bay doors show minor denting on the lower panels. The exterior brickwork is sound but shows surface weathering, and the bay apron has tyre staining.
state.bad: The apparatus bay doors have been replaced with mismatched replacements of different heights. Brickwork mortar is deteriorating above the lintel line, and the bay apron has fractured and settled badly.

description.default: The precinct. An old house, or someone else's old house, depending on the night. Either way, the building remembers where the bodies are buried.

time.morning: Morning briefing filters through the walls. The night's business being handed to the day. What doesn't get mentioned is where the real business hides.
time.afternoon: Interview rooms occupied, case boards updated, the daily machinery of law and order grinding forward. The machinery has weak points.
time.evening: Day shift checking out, night shift checking in. The handover gap is the loosest point of the day.
time.night: Night watch. Fewer officers, lower guard, and the kind of quiet that lets things surface.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The notice board is covered in layers — old flyers buried under new ones. This place runs on paper and habit.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: A certain presence walks in and the room recalibrates. Some people still carry that frequency. The room knows it when it feels it.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The board connects three cases the detective handling them hasn't linked yet. The pattern is there — thirty seconds and it resolves.
attribute.CHARISMA: The desk sergeant produces the file before an explanation is offered. Sometimes an old reputation still clears doors.

gender.male: Old badges don't expire in places like this. A certain kind of man walks in and the room assumes he still carries one.
gender.female: Some of them still don't know how to read a woman in that role. That confusion is a gap. It stays open long enough to matter.

state.good: A substantial civic building with clean masonry and intact entrance glazing. The bollard line at the entrance is straight, newly painted, and securely set.
state.normal: The masonry shows surface weathering and some of the entrance bollards are scuffed. The glazed entrance panels are intact but carry minor scratches.
state.bad: Sections of the masonry have been repointed with mismatched mortar. Several entrance bollards have been removed and the ground sockets filled with concrete. One entrance panel carries a large crack through its glass.

description.default: High walls topped with razor wire rise above every sight line. Watchtowers anchor each corner, their floodlight brackets angled to leave nothing in shadow after dark.

time.morning: The outer perimeter catches the early light flat and indifferent. The walls hold the same colour as the sky before it decides what kind of day it will be.
time.afternoon: The perimeter fence casts its shadow inward. The building mass beyond it absorbs the afternoon heat without returning it.
time.evening: Floodlights begin their cycle as the natural light fades. The compound brightens as the city around it dims.
time.night: The facility is its own island of light. Every lamp is doing exactly the job it was installed to do, and every shadow has been designed out of the compound.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The camera housings track the outer perimeter in overlapping arcs. The blind spots are not accidents — they are where the fence posts break the sight lines.
attribute.STEALTH: The service entrance sits on the north face, away from the main gate. Two cameras, one facing the road, one facing the service lane. Neither covers the gate itself.

gender.male: The compound reads as a solid wall from any approach. No windows below the third level. The upper tiers have narrow apertures set deep into the masonry.
gender.female: The visitor entrance is a separate structure entirely — lower, lighter, with a different grade of glass in the door panels.

state.good: The perimeter fence stands perfectly upright, its tensioned wire clean and without gaps. Freshly painted bollards line the service road and every camera housing is intact.
state.normal: The perimeter fence is sound but shows weathering at the post bases. Several bollards have been scuffed by vehicles and the camera housings show surface rust at their brackets.
state.bad: Sections of perimeter fencing lean at shallow angles, corroded at the post bases. Several camera housing brackets have rusted through and the flood light covers are yellowed and cracked.

description.default: Every letter is a conversation someone had to pay to have. The building holds the record of every one of them.

time.morning: First queue of the day. People who came to collect something that arrived overnight.
time.afternoon: Numbered tickets and long waits. The longer people wait, the more they talk.
time.evening: Counter down to one window. Whatever comes through at this hour, it wasn't sent through official channels.
time.night: Locked. The sorting room runs overnight. Shift workers who see everything and say nothing — until the right question gives them a reason.

attribute.MEMORY: A face at the counter. Handwriting on the parcel at counter three. Both connect to something outside this building and outside this moment.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The parcel addressed to a dead man sits in the collection bay. Third shelf from the bottom. The postmark tells a story.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Routing codes, postmarks, and registered sender addresses are built into every item that passes through this building. The paper trail is a product of the service itself.

gender.male: A man filling in a form, waiting in line. The post office queue renders him invisible. That is its function and its gift.
gender.female: The clerk chats while they process. Postal workers hear things the internet never will. A patient listener gets all of it.

state.good: A clean rendered building with even signage and intact entrance glazing. The entrance step is level and the door furniture polished.
state.normal: The exterior render shows minor crazing and the entrance step has a hairline crack. Signage is intact but the illumination is uneven.
state.bad: The render has delaminated above the entrance and been patched with a mismatched filler. The signage illumination has failed in sections, and the entrance step has a significant crack running to the edge.

description.default: Every answer ever needed was in a library somewhere. People just stopped knowing how to look.

time.morning: First readers of the day. The serious ones, the retired ones, the ones with nowhere else to go and nothing to hide.
time.afternoon: Students crowd the tables with open books and closed expressions. Everyone here is looking for something.
time.evening: The evening quiet is the library at its most honest. Fewer people, better conversations.
time.night: Closed. One light on in the back. Libraries keep their own hours when they think nobody's watching.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The stacks are navigated with precision by those who remember exactly what they read and exactly where the record is wrong.
attribute.MEMORY: Call numbers. Layout. The periodicals archive that hasn't been indexed since the year of the Harmon case. The map of this place is complete.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A book pulled recently from a shelf that hasn't been touched in a decade. Someone else is working the same line of inquiry.
attribute.INTUITION: The silence in here is a shade too deliberate. Someone is listening to what isn't being said.

gender.male: A reference book with no business being relevant to anything current has been pulled and replaced recently. The librarian noticed. They didn't ask.
gender.female: The librarian offers cross-references. Every one taken. Two of them open doors that weren't visible from the entrance.

state.good: A clean stone or brick facade with intact windows and well-maintained entrance steps. The signage is clear and the entrance doors are freshly finished.
state.normal: The facade shows surface weathering and the entrance steps have hairline cracks across several risers. The entrance doors have minor dents on the kick panels.
state.bad: Stone or brickwork has been patched in sections with mismatched material. The entrance steps have significant cracks and one riser has settled noticeably. Several windows have failed seals, showing internal condensation.

description.default: Community on the surface. Underneath, every grievance, alliance and private arrangement the neighbourhood runs on.

time.morning: Yoga classes and coffee mornings. The social glue of a neighbourhood being maintained. And monitored.
time.afternoon: Committee meetings and after-school programmes. Everyone knows everyone's business and nobody says so out loud.
time.evening: The center is at its most alive. Events, classes, socials. The most people, the most noise, the most to overhear.
time.night: Last people locking up. The night's final conversation happens in the car park.

attribute.CHARISMA: Three conversations deep before the coffee urn is reached. People respond to a listener. The room opens up.
attribute.EMPATHY: The meeting upstairs is about a planning dispute. The one downstairs is about the same thing. Neither side knows the other is here.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The crowd mixes regulars and strangers. The ones who stand apart, near the edges of the room, are worth watching.

gender.male: Show up, lend a hand, earn a conversation. Community spaces reward the patient presence. The one who asks for nothing gets offered everything.
gender.female: A woman is welcomed and handed a mug before any introduction. People here share things freely. The room doesn't guard itself.

state.good: A clean rendered or brick building with intact glazing and freshly painted trim. The car park approach is even and clearly marked.
state.normal: The exterior trim paint has begun to chalk and the car park approach surface is starting to crack at the edges. Window frames are intact but showing weathering.
state.bad: Large sections of exterior render have been patched in mismatched tones. The car park approach surface is deeply cracked and potholed, and several window frames have split sills.

description.default: The machine that runs the city. Slow, rusted, and for sale to the right bidder. The bidder's name is somewhere in the building.

time.morning: Officials running early meetings they'll spend the rest of the day denying they had.
time.afternoon: Council sessions and public consultations. The public face of decisions already made.
time.evening: The building empties. A few lights burn on floors they shouldn't. The floor numbers are worth remembering.
time.night: Dark except for the security cameras. The real business of city hall happens in the dark.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Permit records, budget line items, zoning amendments buried on page forty-seven. The truth is always in the paperwork.
attribute.CHARISMA: Departments open to a confident walk and a question that sounds routine. The combination is its own kind of credential.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: The deputy commissioner's office shows the signs of a man under pressure. The file on the contractor is buried but not destroyed.

gender.male: A man who carries himself like an auditor unsettles people. Nobody wants to talk to an auditor. They tell him everything to make him leave.
gender.female: A clerk offers to walk a woman through the filing system. The detour passes the office that actually needed visiting.

state.good: An imposing civic facade of clean stone or render with intact ornamental details. The entrance steps are even and the flagpole fittings are polished.
state.normal: The stone facade shows surface weathering and some ornamental detail has been repointed. The entrance steps are sound but carry scuff marks across the treads.
state.bad: Sections of the ornamental facade have been repaired with plain sealant. Stone treads on the entrance steps have cracked and several have been replaced with concrete substitutes. The flagpole fittings are heavily tarnished.

description.default: Justice — or the paperwork approximation of it. Both have been on display here. The paperwork outlasts them all.

time.morning: Lawyers and defendants funnelling through the metal detectors before the docket opens. Who walks in together tells its own story.
time.afternoon: Full session. Courtroom four has a case that keeps being adjourned. The reason is somewhere in the record.
time.evening: Proceedings winding down. Legal teams gathering in the corridor to say things they wouldn't say in front of a stenographer.
time.night: Locked. Duty officer at the front. The record room sits on an upper floor, behind a door that stays closed tonight.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The body language of three witnesses across two cases maps to a conclusion none of the lawyers have arrived at yet.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: A certain presence in the gallery shifts something in the defendant's posture. That shift is information.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A lawyer passed a note that shouldn't have crossed the aisle. Received one in return that will be denied. Both transactions are on the record.

gender.male: A man in the courthouse reads as counsel or professional — either way, he belongs here. Nobody questions it.
gender.female: A female presence in the gallery reads as invisible to people who've been staring at the same faces day after day. Perfect.

state.good: A solid classical facade with clean stonework and intact glazing in every window. The entrance columns and pediment show no cracks and the paintwork is even.
state.normal: The stonework has surface weathering and some joints have been repointed. The columns are intact but show crazing in the painted finish, and the entrance glazing carries minor scratches.
state.bad: Several stone blocks in the facade have been replaced with mismatched material. Columns show cracking at their bases, and a section of the pediment has been temporarily shored with timber supports.

description.default: Thin walls and thinner excuses. Everything in a building like this gets heard eventually.

time.morning: Work clothes and rushed exits. The morning parade reveals who keeps regular hours and who doesn't.
time.afternoon: Quiet. The working day empties the building. A window of stillness.
time.evening: Cooking smells and argument sounds drifting under doors. The building talks whether anyone's listening or not.
time.night: Still. The stairwell carries noise from three floors away. Sound travels clean in this building.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Some mailboxes overflowing, some buzzers taped over. A building narrates itself if you speak the language.
attribute.STEALTH: Stairs over elevator. Wall-side at each landing. A careful presence moves through this building without leaving a trace.
attribute.EMPATHY: The voices behind these doors tell the story of the building. The pattern is familiar to anyone who knows what to listen for.

gender.male: A man moves through the building with the unremarkable confidence of someone who's supposed to be here. Nobody confirms it. Nobody denies it.
gender.female: A neighbour in the hall holds the door and starts a sentence about the management. The conversation runs six minutes. It contains four leads.

state.good: A neat brick or rendered residential block with intact windows and freshly painted common entrance. The entrance door is solid and the intercom panel is new.
state.normal: The exterior shows general weathering. The common entrance paint is worn around the door handle and the intercom panel has a cracked lens.
state.bad: The exterior render has failed in patches, exposing the substrate beneath. The common entrance door frame is warped and the intercom panel is missing its cover, leaving the wiring exposed.

description.default: Layers of lives stacked on top of each other. What happens on one floor echoes on all of them.

time.morning: Lobby full of people who won't make eye contact. Morning in an apartment building is a masterclass in deliberate blindness.
time.afternoon: Quiet working hours. The building has time to breathe.
time.evening: The tide comes back in. Groceries, gym bags, the smell of every dinner in the city.
time.night: Floor by floor, the lights go out. One doesn't. The number is worth noting.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Fresh scuff on one of the doors. A new lock on a door that had a different lock recently. The building is talking.
attribute.STEALTH: Stairs over elevator, wall-side over centre, steady pace over hurrying. Basic, but it works.
attribute.CHARISMA: The super emerges for a noise complaint on the same floor that needed access. A convenient coincidence.

gender.male: A man rides the elevator without eye contact. The universal signal for leave me alone. It works.
gender.female: The super is helpful in that particular way that means they want to be useful. A woman in a building corridor tends to draw that particular helpfulness.

state.good: A clean multi-storey residential block with intact brick or render cladding and clear window glazing. The entrance canopy is level and the common area lighting is fully functional.
state.normal: The cladding shows general weathering and several window sill details are cracked. The entrance canopy has minor staining from a blocked gutter joint.
state.bad: Cladding panels have delaminated at two corners of the building. Several windows have failed seals with visible internal condensation, and the entrance canopy has a pronounced sag on its leading edge.

description.default: A hundred apartments, three hundred lies, and one address that connects to everything. The floor that matters is somewhere in this building.

time.morning: The lobby revolves with departures. Morning anonymity at its peak.
time.afternoon: Deliveries and maintenance. Faces that belong and faces that don't. The difference is visible to anyone paying attention.
time.evening: Returning residents and food deliveries provide enough traffic to cover movement through the lobby.
time.night: The overnight concierge has the desk. They've been there four hours. Their coffee is cold and their attention is colder.

attribute.PERCEPTION: A building this size runs on patterns. The broken one stands out the moment you know what to look for.
attribute.STEALTH: The delivery window offers three minutes of maximum distraction. More than enough time.
attribute.CHARISMA: A certain ease of bearing reads as long-term residency. No destination, no explanation, no second look.

gender.male: A man nods at the concierge like someone who does this every night. The concierge nods back. Transaction complete.
gender.female: The concierge desk in a large building is the building's nervous system. It tracks everything and everyone that passes through the lobby.

state.good: A tall residential block with clean cladding and intact window glazing across all floors. The entrance lobby glazing is clear and the podium level landscaping is maintained.
state.normal: The cladding shows weathering and differential staining above drainage points. Several upper-floor windows carry tape repairs to hairline cracks in the glazing.
state.bad: Cladding panels have separated at multiple joints across the upper floors, with sealant replacement visible at many seams. Numerous windows have failed seals or cracked panes, and the podium landscaping has been cleared and left as bare concrete.

description.default: Money spent on the illusion of safety. Illusions, of course, never do hold.

time.morning: White-gloved morning service and a lobby so polished the security team's reflection stretches from fifty feet away.
time.afternoon: Quiet, curated, watched. The afternoon hours here carry a weight that resists casual passage.
time.evening: Residents returning in cars worth more than most annual salaries. The lobby earns its marble at this hour.
time.night: The building purrs with the kind of quiet money buys. Nothing in the lobby suggests what's coming.

attribute.CHARISMA: The staff don't question people who look like they belong. It's the oldest rule in a building like this.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Everything here is designed to look unblemished. The crack is always somewhere obvious, for those who haven't been won over by the marble.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Three gaps in the security protocol. All of them visible from the street, if the eye knows where to look.

gender.male: Nobody questions a man who walks in like he owns the lobby. The building's assumptions fit him like a coat.
gender.female: The concierge's assessment takes two seconds longer than it should. Two seconds is enough time for certain kinds of eyes.

state.good: A tall glass and stone residential tower with flawless curtain wall glazing and polished stone podium cladding. Every entrance fixture is clean and the ground-level landscaping is precisely maintained.
state.normal: The curtain wall glazing has isolated pane replacements visible by their slightly different tint. Some of the stone podium cladding has surface scratches, and the landscaping has uneven growth.
state.bad: Multiple curtain wall panels have been replaced with opaque infill units. The stone podium cladding has cracked at several joints, and the ground-floor glazing carries a broken pane boarded with plywood.

description.default: Forty floors of private lives stacked in the sky. Whatever the city holds, something in this stack knows it.

time.morning: Elevators packed, lobby a bottleneck. Everyone moving, nobody watching.
time.afternoon: The complex empties out. The corridors stand empty. A working theory waits to be tested.
time.evening: The building fills back up. The noise covers more than it should.
time.night: The security desk has one person on it. They've been here since eight. Their route is clockwork.

attribute.CHARISMA: The kind of easy conversation that covers three floors' worth of useful information — it starts when someone holds the elevator for a resident.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Hundreds of units and one of them has a different air about it. The number on the door announces itself before the landing ends.
attribute.STEALTH: Anonymous, crowded, and indifferent. A natural habitat for those who prefer not to be found.

gender.male: A name that matches someone in the directory. A walk that says this has been done a thousand times. Nobody checks.
gender.female: What the front desk sees is a resident. That's exactly all they get.

state.good: A campus of tall residential blocks with clean external cladding and intact glazing. The podium-level amenity buildings are well-maintained and the landscaped paths are clear.
state.normal: The blocks show differential weathering, with some facades more stained than others. Sections of the podium paving have cracked and some external light fittings are dark.
state.bad: Several towers show advanced cladding failure, with panels missing or temporary sheeting fixed over failed sections. The podium paving is cracked and heaved, and multiple ground-floor windows are boarded.

description.default: Respectable facades with interesting interiors. Suburbs keep their secrets quieter than the city. Harder to crack. Worth the effort.

time.morning: School runs and commute starts. The street empties fast and stays empty.
time.afternoon: Still and quiet. The kind of quiet that feels watched.
time.evening: Lights behind curtains, the smell of cooking, the sound of lives being lived. The street reads like an open text.
time.night: The row is dark and hushed. A dog barks once and then doesn't. Even the dogs know to keep quiet here.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Three lawns with full wheelie bins. One that's empty. One house that clearly hasn't been occupied lately.
attribute.EMPATHY: The neighbourhood has a tension under its orderly surface. One house three doors down accounts for most of it.
attribute.STEALTH: The pavement rewards the unhurried pace of someone without a destination. Nothing here triggers suspicion in the idle observer.

gender.male: A man walks it once like he's cutting through. The second pass is something else.
gender.female: A resident watering their garden says hello. Neighbourhoods like this remember women they haven't seen before. The encounter, for those who know how to use it, is already useful.

state.good: A terrace of well-maintained brick or render houses with matching door colours and intact roof lines. The shared boundary walls are even and the front paths are clear.
state.normal: The terrace shows individual variation in condition. Some houses have fresh paint while others are weathered. The shared boundary walls are intact but some coping stones are cracked.
state.bad: Several houses in the terrace have visibly deteriorating facades. Peeling paint and open joints in the brickwork are common, and the shared boundary walls have sections that have settled and cracked badly.

description.default: Owner-occupied, well-maintained, and under the impression that nothing bad happens here. The impression is wrong.

time.morning: The morning lobby rush is brief and efficient. People who own their apartments have somewhere to be.
time.afternoon: The building quiets after ten a.m. Movement shows. So does anything that breaks the quiet.
time.evening: Returns from the workday. The concierge reads the evening crowd better than most. The crowd, in turn, is being read.
time.night: Night-mode lobby. One desk lamp and a camera covering the south wall.

attribute.CHARISMA: The concierge has a practiced way with long-term residents — an easy familiarity built on years of small interactions. Buildings like this reward familiarity.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A reflection in the lobby mirror places someone on a floor they haven't been assigned.
attribute.STEALTH: Shift timing. Blind-spot rotation. Entry window: ninety seconds. The margins, for the practiced eye, are generous.

gender.male: A newspaper folded under one arm. A measured walk to the elevator. Man heading up — nobody's problem.
gender.female: The concierge is chattier than their job requires. Small talk, in the right hands, trades for building access.

state.good: A clean residential block with uniform cladding and intact balcony railings across all levels. The entrance canopy is level and the building signage is freshly illuminated.
state.normal: The exterior cladding shows general weathering and the balcony railings have surface rust on their fixing points. The entrance canopy is structurally sound but stained.
state.bad: The cladding has failed at several joints, and sections have been replaced with mismatched panels. Balcony railings at multiple levels are heavily corroded, and some have been removed and replaced with temporary boarding.

description.default: The old and the overlooked. The city filed them away decades ago. That means they saw everything the city wanted forgotten.

time.morning: Routine runs deep here. Medications, meals, the quiet discipline of lives that have been lived long enough to know what matters.
time.afternoon: Visiting hours and activity sessions. The building is at its most porous. The traffic is useful to those who know how to read it.
time.evening: Dinner in the common room. The most social hour of the facility's day. The most talkative.
time.night: Night staff, soft rounds, the hum of institutional care. And one resident who still keeps strange hours.

attribute.EMPATHY: In this building, sitting and listening without checking the time is worth more than a badge.
attribute.MEMORY: A resident mentions a name from forty years ago. The first recognition of that name in decades changes the conversation.
attribute.CHARISMA: Staff and residents warm quickly to those with the manner of someone who means it. They've earned the right to know the difference.

gender.male: A chair pulled to the edge of the common room, a patient posture, and an open ear. The room comes to those who wait for it in a place like this.
gender.female: The warmth extended here is the particular kind reserved for people who feel safe. Whatever comes after, they feel right about it.

state.good: A clean, low-rise building with accessible paths, even paving, and well-maintained rendered walls. The accessible ramp surfaces are non-slip and clearly marked.
state.normal: The exterior render shows crazing and the accessible path paving has minor cracks. The accessible ramp edge markings have faded but are still visible.
state.bad: The exterior render has failed in patches and several accessible path sections have heaved, creating uneven transitions. The accessible ramp surface has cracked and one edge marking strip has detached.

description.default: Thin walls and cheaper regrets. Every budget hotel is a short story collection that nobody's proud of.

time.morning: The breakfast queue is a parade of people who slept badly and still have to function. The clientele has that look.
time.afternoon: The lobby is transit — everyone passing through, nobody staying.
time.evening: Check-in rush. One overworked clerk, a queue that tests patience. The wait is long enough to catalogue who else is checking in.
time.night: Somewhere down the hall a door slams. Somewhere else, a call ends badly. The building tells its secrets to anyone willing to listen.

attribute.PERCEPTION: One of the rooms has been entered in the last hour. The latch is set wrong. Whoever's in there didn't grow up in hotels.
attribute.STEALTH: Indifferent staff and anonymous guests. This place was built for people who don't want to be found.
attribute.MEMORY: Every budget hotel like this one follows the same template. Same layout, same broken ice machine, same smell. The floor plan announces itself.

gender.male: Cash at a budget hotel is unremarkable. The clerk processes a hundred transactions a day and applies the same level of scrutiny to each one.
gender.female: The night clerk is more attentive than the job requires. A smile, deployed precisely, is currency here.

state.good: A clean rendered or cladded building with intact signage and clear entrance glazing. The car park surface is even and the entrance canopy is level.
state.normal: The exterior cladding shows surface weathering and the entrance canopy has minor staining. The signage is legible but the illumination is uneven.
state.bad: Cladding panels have delaminated at the corners and been patched with visible sealant. The entrance canopy has a pronounced lean, and the signage has multiple dark sections.

description.default: Business travel: everyone with somewhere to be and something to hide. The lobby is full of people who all belong here and none of them do.

time.morning: Laptop screens and phone calls at breakfast. The most productive lies of anyone's day happen here before nine.
time.afternoon: The lobby doubles as a meeting room for deals that aren't happening in the conference centre.
time.evening: The bar fills with people unwinding from the performance of being professional. Loosened ties, loosened tongues.
time.night: Corporate quiet. The hallways smell like carpet and confidentiality agreements.

attribute.CHARISMA: The front desk upgrades without being asked. The look of someone who expects it is unmistakable.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The conference attendance list posted at the concierge desk maps who is in town, what organisation they represent, and which floors they have access to.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The room's sight lines, the corridor camera, and the stairwell exit — all catalogued before the porter finishes the room tour.

gender.male: The efficiency of a man who has checked in a hundred times. The name on the register isn't always the same name.
gender.female: Check-in handled in forty-five seconds flat. The clerk adds an unrequested amenity regardless. The amenity comes with floor access that wasn't on the original booking.

state.good: A smart facade of clean stone or cladding with polished entrance fixtures and clear curtain wall glazing. The entrance forecourt is even and freshly marked.
state.normal: The facade cladding shows surface weathering and the curtain wall has isolated pane replacements. The entrance forecourt has minor cracking at the drainage channels.
state.bad: The facade cladding has cracked and delaminated at several joints. The curtain wall has multiple replaced panes of differing tints, and the entrance forecourt surface is uneven and heavily stained.

description.default: Marble and misdirection. A luxury hotel is the best place in the city to conduct business that can't be traced.

time.morning: White-glove breakfast service. The conversation at table three started early and will cost someone dearly by noon.
time.afternoon: The hotel holds its breath in the afternoon. Everything about it says: nothing happens here. Everything about it is lying.
time.evening: Full dining room, full bar. Everyone dressed for someone else's benefit.
time.night: The hotel turns to silk and shadows. The real conversations happen now.

attribute.CHARISMA: The luxury hotel concierge maintains a near-perfect memory for regular guests. Long-term repeat visitors are known by preference, habit, and quiet expectation.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every exchange here is a transaction. What's being bought and sold at each table is legible to the right eye.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The guest list, the room assignments, the kitchen supplier with the unmarked van — all three connect to the same name.

gender.male: The hotel's assumptions fit like a second suit. Nobody questions the man at the corner table who hasn't ordered yet.
gender.female: A woman dining alone in the bar at this hour is either very confident or very dangerous. The distinction isn't always visible from the outside.

state.good: An immaculate facade of dressed stone and polished metal fixtures. Every surface reflects careful maintenance, from the clean curtain wall glazing to the even stone forecourt paving.
state.normal: The stone facade shows surface weathering at the joints and some metal fixtures have dulled. The forecourt paving is sound but carries minor staining at the drain points.
state.bad: Stone facing panels have cracked at several joints and mortar repairs are visible from the street. Polished metal fixtures have tarnished significantly, and a section of the forecourt paving has been replaced with mismatched stone.

description.default: Small office, small secrets — but they're the kind that fit in a briefcase and travel well.

time.morning: Staff arriving, coffee in hand, guard not yet up. The freshest intelligence of the day is at nine a.m.
time.afternoon: Post-lunch slowdown. Everyone's a little more honest when they're a little more tired.
time.evening: Most of the floor dark by six. One office still lit. That office is worth watching.
time.night: Empty. A single light on the top floor. That's not the cleaner.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The room assignments. The name on the door that doesn't appear in the staff directory. The memo nobody was meant to leave out. Three minutes inside and all of it is visible.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Someone left a printout on the shared printer that shouldn't be shared. It sits there in plain sight.
attribute.STEALTH: Open-plan office, nobody watching the edges. A figure moving through with the unhurried air of a consultant on a walkthrough raises no alarms.

gender.male: A man walking the floor like he owns the contract. Assumptions run in his favour.
gender.female: Past the reception desk before anyone thinks to ask. Purpose is its own credential.

state.good: A clean rendered or brick building with intact window glazing and freshly painted entrance. The accessible path and car park approach are clearly marked.
state.normal: The exterior shows general weathering and the entrance paint is worn around the door furniture. Window sills are intact but showing surface crazing.
state.bad: The render has failed above several windows and been patched with sealant. The entrance door frame is swollen out of true, and the car park approach has cracked and settled badly.

description.default: Mid-size offices, mid-size lies. The crimes here are the unglamorous kind — fraud, cover-ups, the daily corruption of small decisions.

time.morning: The building wakes with the particular energy of people about to spend eight hours being someone else.
time.afternoon: Conference rooms booked solid, hallways full of conversations that stop at a certain kind of footstep. Enough has already been heard.
time.evening: The building drains by six. Whatever's still running at seven has a reason.
time.night: Security rounds and a cleaning crew. There is a gap between them.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The directory board tells the official story. The gaps in it tell the real one.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The elevator indicator shows an upper floor. Nobody has a meeting there at this time of day.
attribute.EMPATHY: The staff on this floor are performing calm. Performed calm has a cost, and it shows in the eyes of those who know what to look for.

gender.male: The floor carried like it's been briefed. Nobody wants to be the one who asks questions.
gender.female: The authority of the last person who was right. Nobody argues with it.

state.good: A clean mid-rise commercial building with even cladding and intact curtain wall glazing. The entrance forecourt is level and the building signage is clearly illuminated.
state.normal: The cladding shows differential weathering and the curtain wall has isolated pane replacements. The entrance forecourt paving has minor cracks at the expansion joints.
state.bad: The cladding has delaminated at several upper-floor corners and temporary fixing bolts are visible. Multiple curtain wall panes have been replaced with opaque infill, and the entrance forecourt surface is badly cracked.

description.default: A thousand people and one secret that ties all of them together. It's in here somewhere.

time.morning: The lobby revolves with the morning crush. Anonymity at its peak. The tide carries everyone.
time.afternoon: The building runs at full capacity. Noise, movement, and the comfortable cover of normalcy.
time.evening: The floor drains fast after five. The ones who stay are the ones worth watching.
