time.night: Cleaners and security. Two schedules. One window between them.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: A building this size runs on internal structure. The structure reads in the titles on doors, the deference in hallways, and the unattended desk with papers left out.
attribute.CHARISMA: A visitor's badge that should slow things down. A manner that says otherwise.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The executive elevator shows recent activity for a floor that's been officially off-limits. Nobody flagged the access.

gender.male: The walk of a person with clearance. The assumption does the rest.
gender.female: The walk tailored to match the floor. The floor opens accordingly.

state.good: A substantial commercial tower with clean curtain wall glazing and even stone or concrete base cladding. The entrance plaza is level and the building identification signage is fully illuminated.
state.normal: The curtain wall has isolated pane replacements visible by their different tint. The base cladding shows surface scratches and the entrance plaza paving has cracked at the perimeter.
state.bad: Multiple curtain wall panels have failed and been replaced with temporary opaque infill. The base cladding has cracked joints and one section has been shored with a temporary steel prop. The entrance plaza paving is badly heaved and cracked.

description.default: The top floor of everything wrong with the city. This is where decisions get laundered and consequences get delegated.

time.morning: The executive floors have been active since before the sun cleared the skyline. The kind of decisions made at dawn are never the comfortable kind.
time.afternoon: Board presentations and media management. Every word calculated for an audience that may include unwelcome observers, whether they know it or not.
time.evening: Networking events and private dinners. The business of the day continues under different lighting.
time.night: The servers never sleep. The building holds its breath. Something is always watching.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Power has a grammar. The fluent speaker knows exactly where the sentences fall apart.
attribute.CHARISMA: Senior executives open doors for people who look like they're about to open them anyway. The look is a readable thing.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: Without a raised voice or a broken smile, it is made perfectly clear: this one will not be managed.

gender.male: A boardroom full of men in suits assumes any arriving man is one of them. The assumption holds for roughly twelve minutes.
gender.female: The room underestimates and overestimates itself in equal measure. The equation has been corrected before.

state.good: A prestige commercial building with immaculate curtain wall glazing and precisely detailed stone or metal base cladding. The entrance plaza is uniform and all signage is fully illuminated.
state.normal: The curtain wall has a few isolated pane replacements and the stone base cladding shows surface weathering. The entrance plaza has minor cracking at the expansion joints.
state.bad: Significant curtain wall pane failures have resulted in multiple opaque infill replacements across several floors. The stone base cladding has cracked in sections and one corner has been shored with temporary steelwork. The entrance plaza is partly closed for repairs.

description.default: Shared desks and the illusion of community. Everyone working independently and desperately hoping someone notices.

time.morning: First ones in are claiming territory. Laptops open, headphones on, eyes everywhere.
time.afternoon: The space hits critical density. Pitches, calls, the low-grade performance of productivity.
time.evening: Product launches and meetups. The noise peaks and the guard drops. The hour belongs to anyone paying attention.
time.night: Empty desks and dormant screens. Whatever was built here today will be dismantled somewhere else tomorrow.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Three pitches visible from one seat. Two wireframes. One conversation about a funding round that shouldn't be public yet.
attribute.CHARISMA: People introduce themselves before the bag is unpacked. The social density of this place rewards arrival.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Coworking spaces are porous by design. What's supposedly private here is only private to people who aren't paying attention.

gender.male: A hot desk near the back. A laptop opened without ceremony. Nobody here knows anyone well enough to notice a new face.
gender.female: A woman settling in at a hot desk gets an introduction offer inside thirty seconds. Social capital accrues fast in places like this.

state.good: A clean, bright shopfront or commercial suite facade with clear glazing and crisp signage. The entrance is accessible and the window displays are tidy.
state.normal: The exterior shows minor weathering and the signage has started to fade. The entrance threshold is intact but has minor chips at the edge.
state.bad: The facade cladding has blistered and peeled in sections. The signage is partially dark and the entrance glazing has a cracked pane held together with tape.

description.default: A string of businesses held together by a parking lot and low expectations. Somebody's running something here that isn't on the signage.

time.morning: Half the shutters aren't up yet. The ones that are open early have a reason to be.
time.afternoon: Foot traffic and errands. The strip mall at its most normal, which means the least guarded.
time.evening: Most shops closing. The ones staying open after dark are the ones worth watching.
time.night: One light still on at the far end. Somebody's burning the midnight oil, or just the electricity.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Twelve storefronts and one of them has inventory coming in after midnight. The kind of anomaly that doesn't go unnoticed.
attribute.CHARISMA: Business cards change hands across three storefronts inside of ten minutes. The people here talk. Everybody's running an angle and everybody needs somebody else's.
attribute.STEALTH: The foot traffic along the strip runs continuous. Faces pass without registering. The crowd swallows what it doesn't notice.

gender.male: Men move through the strip with a list in hand or the look of one. Errands are the oldest cover story.
gender.female: A woman pausing at a display window reads as background noise. The oldest cover in the book, and it has never been retired.

state.good: A consistent line of clean shopfronts under a level canopy. The shared walkway is freshly swept and the fascia boards are uniformly painted.
state.normal: Several tenant signs have been replaced individually, breaking the uniformity of the fascia. The walkway surface is intact but stained near the drain outlets.
state.bad: Empty unit spaces behind boarded fronts break the run of the shopfronts. The canopy has buckled in sections and the fascia boards carry peeling paint in large strips.

description.default: Consumption as cover. A thousand people who aren't watching where they're going, and one who is.

time.morning: The center's barely awake. Staff outnumber shoppers and everyone is running on autopilot.
time.afternoon: Peak hours. The crowd is the cover. Faces disappear into it without effort.
time.evening: Last rush before closing. People in a hurry make mistakes. The pace of the room does the rest.
time.night: Security walks the dark concourse. Their route runs in tight, predictable loops. The pattern repeats.

attribute.CHARISMA: The regulars move through it with ease and people respond accordingly — a nod here, a conversation there. Familiarity opens doors faster than credentials.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Exit signs mark three ground-floor routes. Someone near the food court hasn't touched their tray in ten minutes. They're not here to eat.
attribute.STEALTH: A face matched to the crowd's pace disappears into it. The furniture notices nothing.

gender.male: A stationary man in a shopping centre draws attention. A moving one becomes part of the scenery.
gender.female: A woman moving through the centre reads as background noise. Just another shopper. Everybody's blind spot.

state.good: A broad facade of clean cladding panels and clear entrance glazing. The landscaped approaches are tidy and the entrance canopy is level.
state.normal: The cladding panels are intact but some have faded unevenly. The entrance glazing carries minor scratches at knee height, and several exterior light fittings are dark.
state.bad: Loose cladding panels rattle at the upper levels, and some have been removed entirely. The entrance canopy has developed a visible sag, and the car park approaches are rutted and cracked.

description.default: The regional mall: where ambition goes to die and foot traffic goes to multiply. The crowd has its own momentum, and it doesn't care about anyone's agenda.

time.morning: Mall walkers in their circuit, the early stores cranking up. The lull before the storm leaves the floor open and the angles clean.
time.afternoon: Packed. Every escalator a river of bodies. One figure moves against the current — the only one who seems to know exactly where they're going.
time.evening: Last hour of trading. The security rotation thins and the pace picks up. Things happen in the last hour.
time.night: The big dark. Emergency lighting and a security patrol that runs like clockwork. Predictable down to the minute.

attribute.CHARISMA: Conversations start by accident in a crowd this thick. The right ones end with what someone came for.
attribute.PERCEPTION: In a crowd this size, finding the wrong person is easy once you know what wrong looks like.
attribute.STEALTH: This many people, this much noise. A face could vanish into the food court for a week and nobody would blink.

gender.male: A man moving with purpose through the browsing mass reads as someone who knows where he's going. The crowd makes way.
gender.female: In a place this size, a woman moving without urgency becomes functionally invisible. The mall doesn't differentiate.

state.good: An expansive facade of clean stone aggregate and glass curtain wall. The entrance towers are fully lit and the exterior signage is uniform.
state.normal: The exterior stone shows weathering at the seams and the glass curtain wall has isolated pane replacements visible by their cleaner colour. Several entrance fixtures are dark.
state.bad: Sections of the stone aggregate cladding have detached, leaving exposed substrate visible. The glass curtain wall has boarded panes at multiple levels and the entrance towers carry large rust stains from corroding fixings.

description.default: Aisles and angles. Everything in a supermarket is designed to move people in one direction. The layout is a quiet argument about control.

time.morning: Freshly stocked and barely staffed. The morning crowd is methodical. The aisles run clean and uncluttered.
time.afternoon: Busy aisles and distracted shoppers. Midday is when the delivery drivers come in the back. Worth noting.
time.evening: The after-work rush puts everyone in a hurry and nobody watching the edges.
time.night: Night-shift restocking in near-silence. The back corridors open up. Interesting.

attribute.PERCEPTION: There's a gap on the shelf that the stockist hasn't flagged yet. Something moved through here and didn't get logged. The absence says more than the inventory.
attribute.MEMORY: The prices on the back shelf sit below chain average, and have for a while. Distribution chains leave marks when they change. The marks are here.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Empty shelves aren't always out of stock. Sometimes they're a story nobody wants to tell.

gender.male: A man with a cart in a supermarket reads as furniture. Nobody questions it. The cart is practically camouflage.
gender.female: The floor manager offers a friendly nod to a woman near the back aisle. Women ask questions in supermarkets all the time. Nobody reads anything into it.

state.good: A wide, clean retail facade with evenly lit signage and clear glazed frontage. The loading dock approach is freshly marked and drained.
state.normal: The facade cladding is intact but some panels have faded unevenly in the sun. The loading dock is stained and the exterior trolley bays are dented.
state.bad: The facade panels have warped at the upper fascia, and the signage illumination is patchy. The loading dock area is heavily cracked and oil-stained, and the external drainage gutter has separated from the wall.

description.default: Everything sold in bulk, including whatever the back room doesn't advertise. The fine print is what this place was built on.

time.morning: The store opens thin. The aisles carry sound cleanly — two conversations audible from three rows away. No crowd to absorb what shouldn't be heard.
time.afternoon: Forklifts and noise. Good cover for what moves below the surface. The walls are close but the sounds are closer.
time.evening: Thinning out. Easier to work but easier to be seen. Double-edged.
time.night: Closed. The loading bay out back is a different story.

attribute.STRENGTH: The scale of the place is built for weight — industrial shelving, pallet loads, goods measured by the ton. The floor doesn't yield.
attribute.STAMINA: The floor runs long between departments. The walk from receiving to the back office covers ground most storefronts never see.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One pallet moved recently but logged as untouched. The discrepancy is visible in the documentation.

gender.male: A man in the trade section reads as routine. The posture carries credentials that nobody stops to check.
gender.female: The floor rep crosses the aisle to assist. Three questions get answered freely. The fourth answer is somewhere in the first three.

state.good: A large-format steel panel building with clean soffits and fresh exterior paint. Signage is fully lit and the apron is flat and unmarked.
state.normal: The steel panels have surface rust at the lower joints. The signage is legible but the illumination flickers in one section. Tyre marks score the apron.
state.bad: Corrosion has spread from the lower panels upward, and several sections have been patched with mismatched steel. The apron is heavily cracked and potholed, and the overhead signage is partially dark.

description.default: Cheap meals and the kind of conversation that happens when people stop being careful. The chairs are always warm.

time.morning: Eggs and alibis. Breakfast clients say what they mean because the day hasn't taught them caution yet.
time.afternoon: Lunch crowd, overworked and undertipped. Everyone wants to vent. The room runs on frustrated energy looking for an ear.
time.evening: Tables full of people saying things they'll regret later. Perfect.
time.night: The kitchen's shutting down. Last round of drinks, last round of honesty.

attribute.CHARISMA: The corner booth in the back is the most valuable table in the room. It fills quickly with people who want to see the room without being seen from it.
attribute.EMPATHY: Two arguments brewing at separate tables. One reconciliation in the corner that has the posture of something that won't hold.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Three minutes at a table and the room is already mapped — two entrances, one service corridor, a fire exit behind the kitchen that sits propped open.

gender.male: A man nursing a coffee in a corner booth is invisible. That's the point, and the room treats him accordingly.
gender.female: The waiter is attentive in ways that overstep the job description. The attention gets redirected. A useful conversation emerges from the wreckage.

state.good: A clean rendered exterior with an intact awning and spotless windows. The entrance planters are freshly maintained and the door hardware is bright.
state.normal: The exterior paint is intact but shows crazing near the window reveals. The awning has begun to fade on its leading edge and one entrance planter has cracked.
state.bad: The render has delaminated in patches above the windows. The awning fabric has split along one seam, and the entrance step has sunk and cracked away from the threshold.

description.default: Crystal and candlelight draped over the same old lies. The prices go up but the sins stay affordable.

time.morning: The restaurant is dark and silent. Only prep staff working the stock. They see more than the dining room crowd ever does.
time.afternoon: Quiet lunch service. Well-dressed regulars conducting business that belongs in a back room.
time.evening: Full house. Every table is a transaction. The evening is a ledger — who owes whom, and what the debt costs.
time.night: The last cover lingers. The staff wants to go home. Someone at table nine doesn't want to be overheard.

attribute.CHARISMA: The maitre d' decides in five seconds that a face is worth accommodating. The decision is visible in the posture — the chair moved out, the menu offered first.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The wine list, the menu pricing, the clientele — a front for something. The seams show if the light is right.
attribute.EMPATHY: Table six has been smiling all evening. The body language says the conversation ended badly three courses ago.

gender.male: A man who orders expensive and doesn't eat is either nervous or important. Either reads well in a room like this.
gender.female: A woman dining alone in a place like this reads as either very confident or very dangerous. The room can't decide which and doesn't stop watching.

state.good: A polished stone facade with clean brass fixtures and unscratched sidelight glazing. The canopy is taut, its lettering crisp and fully illuminated.
state.normal: The stone facade has dulled with weathering and the brass fixtures show the beginnings of tarnish. The canopy is structurally sound but one illuminated letter flickers.
state.bad: Stone facing panels have separated at the mortar joints. The brass fixtures are heavily tarnished and one is missing entirely. The canopy frame has sagged, pulling the fabric covering into folds.

description.default: Dark rooms and people who came here to stop thinking. Not everyone in the seats is here for the film.

time.morning: First screening of the day. Retirees and insomniacs who didn't go home. Honest company.
time.afternoon: Matinees for families. Whatever's happening in the back row stays invisible to the crowd.
time.evening: Prime time. A hundred people looking at the same screen. None of them watching the exits.
time.night: Late showing, sparse crowd, and a man two rows ahead who hasn't looked at the screen once.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The camera blind spot in the lobby announces itself before the doors open. The geometry is generous.
attribute.MEMORY: This film has been seen before. The man sitting in the same seat as last time has not.
attribute.STEALTH: The dark is an advantage that requires no reminder.

gender.male: A ticket purchased for one screen. A seat taken in another. Nobody connects the dots.
gender.female: The aisle seat. Old habit. The kind that keeps certain people alive.

state.good: A bold facade with clean render or cladding and fully illuminated marquee and signage. The entrance forecourt is clear and the external poster frames are intact.
state.normal: The facade shows surface weathering and several marquee letters are slightly dimmer than the rest. The entrance forecourt has minor cracking and one poster frame has a broken hinge.
state.bad: The marquee lighting has failed in large sections and the facade render has cracked and stained. Several poster frames are missing panels and the entrance forecourt is deeply cracked and patched with cold-lay asphalt.

description.default: Pins and noise and the democratic misery of rented shoes. Everybody's equal in a bowling alley. That makes it useful.

time.morning: League morning. Regulars who've been showing up for decades. They've seen more than they admit.
time.afternoon: Families and birthday parties. Loud, distracted, good cover.
time.evening: The bar is doing real business now. People bowl worse when they're drinking and talk better.
time.night: Cosmic bowling. Black lights and music turned up past the point of caution. The night shift has different rules.

attribute.AGILITY: Whoever's at the line doesn't throw like a civilian. The lane seven crowd goes quiet.
attribute.CHARISMA: A lane share offer arrives before the shoes are paid for. Social currency works fast in places like this.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The group at lane eleven hasn't bowled once. They're here to talk to someone. That someone hasn't arrived yet.

gender.male: The lane invite accepted. Two games. By the end of the second, the conversation has delivered what was needed.
gender.female: Underestimated at lane four. The score by the ninth frame corrects the record.

state.good: A large commercial building with clean render or cladding and fully illuminated signage. The entrance canopy is level and the car park approach is clearly marked.
state.normal: The exterior cladding shows general weathering and the signage has one dark letter. The entrance canopy is structurally sound but stained, and the car park markings are faded.
state.bad: The facade cladding has delaminated in sections and been patched with mismatched material. The signage illumination has failed in large portions, and the car park approach is cracked and potholed.

description.default: Dark, loud, and built on the premise that nobody will remember what they did here. Somebody always does.

time.morning: Doors locked, lights up, cleaners sweeping up the remains of other people's bad decisions.
time.afternoon: The club is a dead room in daylight. Staff prep and the particular hangover of a place that only makes sense after midnight.
time.evening: Pre-opening. Sound checks and staff briefings. The performance hasn't started. Neither has the trouble.
time.night: The music lands before the door's fully open. This is the only time this place tells the truth.

attribute.CHARISMA: The VIP corridor opens without a conversation. The right frequency for rooms like this — someone has it.
attribute.STEALTH: A figure moves through the crowd and leaves no impression on anyone. Not absence of identity — deliberate erasure.
attribute.INTUITION: The energy in this room has an undercurrent the regular crowd hasn't caught yet. It's there before it's visible.

gender.male: The door staff step aside. The right kind of confidence reads in any lighting.
gender.female: Access is quick, which is its own kind of problem. Back to something solid, eyes moving — that's the calculus of a woman in a room like this.

state.good: A clean facade with intact neon or LED signage and clear entrance glazing. The queue barriers are new and the entrance canopy is level.
state.normal: The exterior shows general weathering and some signage elements are dimmer than others. Queue barrier posts are scuffed and the entrance canopy has minor staining.
state.bad: Large sections of the signage are dark and the facade has been tagged with graffiti that has been partly removed, leaving ghost marks. The entrance canopy has a visible sag and queue barrier posts are bent.

description.default: Sixty thousand people and one of them knows where the body went. One in sixty thousand. Someone's counting.

time.morning: Empty stands and groundskeepers. The arena stripped of its performance, just structure and maintenance. Easier to work.
time.afternoon: Pre-game activity building. The crowd filing in is the best place to find someone who doesn't want to be found.
time.evening: The main event. The noise, the energy, the cover of sixty thousand people looking somewhere else.
time.night: Post-game dispersal. The crowd thins and the arena resets. The people who stay after are the ones worth watching.

attribute.STRENGTH: The physical performance on the field reads differently to someone who understands exactly what it costs.
attribute.STAMINA: The crowd, the noise, the pressure — it wires most people up and wears them down. Some people run on it.
attribute.AGILITY: A figure moves through a packed crowd the way a fish moves through water. Nobody notices. Nobody's supposed to.

gender.male: Unremarkable in a crowd like this — which is the only thing worth being.
gender.female: The sight lines the crowd creates run one way. Sixty thousand people looking one direction means nobody's watching the other.

state.good: A massive clean-clad venue with intact glazed atria and fully illuminated external signage. The external concourse paving is even and freshly marked.
state.normal: The cladding shows weathering at the lower levels where it is within reach. Some external signage elements are dimmer than others and the concourse paving has hairline cracks.
state.bad: Cladding panels at the lower levels have been damaged and removed or replaced with mismatched sections. Multiple signage elements are dark and the concourse paving is badly cracked and patched.

description.default: Loading bays and blind spots. Warehouses keep the city's secrets stacked on shelves and wrapped in plastic.

time.morning: Delivery vans and morning manifests. The paperwork that comes in with the first pallet is worth reading.
time.afternoon: Pick and pack, in and out. The rhythm of honest work covering whatever isn't.
time.evening: Last shipments dispatched. The dock crew wants to go home. Tired people make useful mistakes.
time.night: Locked up and alarmed. Except for the dock bay on the east side that was deliberately not alarmed.

attribute.STRENGTH: Heavy freight and high shelving — the physical language of this place. Someone here speaks it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The inventory sheets don't match the shelf count. Someone's moving product through here without logging it.
attribute.STEALTH: Racking twelve feet high on every side. An operation could run from in here and nobody would know where to look.

gender.male: A clipboard lifted from the dispatch desk, someone checking something. The look of it is enough to go unchallenged.
gender.female: The shift supervisor does a double take and then gets helpful fast. The answers are in before the helpfulness runs out.

state.good: A clean steel-framed building with straight walls and intact roof sheeting. The roller shutter doors close true and the apron is clear and unmarked.
state.normal: Surface rust is visible at the lower wall joints. The roller shutter doors close with minor binding and the apron has tyre staining and a few shallow cracks.
state.bad: Rust has spread across the lower wall panels and some sheeting has pulled away from the roof line. The roller shutter doors stick open or closed, and the apron concrete has fractured and heaved.

description.default: Two hundred thousand square feet of things that aren't what they're labelled. A building built on the gap between contents and description.

time.morning: Forklifts and shift briefings. The morning's first movements reveal the overnight's real activity.
time.afternoon: Peak operation. Noise, machinery, the legitimate chaos that conceals the other kind.
time.evening: The day shift hands over with a thirty-minute window where nobody owns the floor. A window.
time.night: Automated lines and skeleton crew. The building running on its own, accountable to nothing.

attribute.STRENGTH: The physical scale of this place earns a different kind of respect. Some people have already earned it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One aisle hasn't been logged but the shelf dust is fresh. Something was here and moved quickly.
attribute.STEALTH: A building this large, this loud, this busy. A figure here is a footnote in the footnotes.

gender.male: The high-vis hanging by the door gets taken. Someone walks the aisles like they're doing a stock count.
gender.female: The floor manager appears fast. Questions are ready on both sides. One set is better.

state.good: A large steel-clad industrial building with clean roof sheeting and straight walls. The roller shutter bays are all operative and the loading apron is freshly marked.
state.normal: Surface rust is visible at the wall base and some roof sheets have minor repairs with sealant tape. The loading apron shows tyre scoring and some minor cracking.
state.bad: The lower wall sheeting is heavily corroded and several panels have been replaced with mismatched material. Roof sheeting has lifted at the ridge on one side and the loading apron is cracked and broken in several areas.

description.default: The grind of machines making things the city uses without asking how. Someone is here to ask how.

time.morning: Day shift clocking in, machines warming up. The changeover is where the conversations live.
time.afternoon: Full production. The noise makes everything else inaudible. Good for a conversation not meant to be overheard.
time.evening: Evening shift rotation. Two crews, two sets of information. Only one is needed.
time.night: Automated lines running without supervision. The humans are gone. Something else is using the building.

attribute.STRENGTH: Honest, physical work. The language of this floor has no room for condescension.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The production schedule, the supply invoices, the deviation logs — the machinery of this place is hiding something in the numbers.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One line is running out of calibration. It's been running that way for weeks. Someone told it to.

gender.male: The floor extends a flat nod to the presence — the acknowledgment of people who assume reason to be here. The reason exists.
gender.female: The supervisor comes over to manage the situation. The managing ends with answers given.

state.good: A clean industrial complex with straight external walls, intact roof sheeting, and clearly marked vehicle routes. Exhaust and utility penetrations are cleanly flashed.
state.normal: The exterior walls show general weathering and surface rust at exposed metalwork. Vehicle route markings are faded and some external utility penetrations have been resealed with visible patches.
state.bad: External wall cladding has corroded significantly at the lower sections. Roof sheeting has lifted at several points and temporary repairs are visible. Vehicle routes are barely traceable on the cracked and stained yard surface.

description.default: The city's memory stored on racks in a cold room. Every secret that ever touched a network passed through a building like this.

time.morning: Day team running diagnostics on overnight alerts. Whatever the night shift caught sits in a queue, waiting.
time.afternoon: The hum of servers and the cold of precision climate control. The most honest room in the city: it never stops recording.
time.evening: Maintenance windows. Controlled access. Someone always has to be in the building during a maintenance window.
time.night: The facility runs without daylight. The servers don't sleep. Neither does the work.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Rack layout, cooling architecture, cable management — the infrastructure reads like a blueprint of modern ambition.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One rack is running three degrees warmer than its neighbours. That's not a cooling fault. That's activity.
attribute.STEALTH: Moving with the certainty of someone who belongs here. Certainty is most of what keycard access buys anyway.

gender.male: A badge presented with the posture of an engineer who's been here a hundred times. The desk accepts the performance.
gender.female: The on-duty engineer is thorough. Each question answered accurately enough to pass, vaguely enough to stay mobile.

state.good: A clean, secure-looking building with unscratched external cladding and intact perimeter security fencing. All external mechanical plant housings are intact and level.
state.normal: The external cladding shows minor weathering and some fixings have surface rust. The perimeter security fencing is intact but shows wear at the gates. External plant housings are sound.
state.bad: External cladding has cracked and lifted at two corners of the building. The perimeter security fencing has been repaired in sections with mismatched material, and several external plant housings are dented and misaligned.

description.default: Everything people can't throw away and won't admit to keeping. The private archaeology of a life. One of these units is waiting to be cracked.

time.morning: Early access before the day gets complicated. People retrieving things they need urgently don't look casual.
time.afternoon: Regular traffic, regular faces — and one that doesn't belong to the pattern.
time.evening: Last entry before the after-hours lockdown. Whatever someone needs that urgently, it couldn't wait.
time.night: Access code required. Corridors empty and lit by motion sensors. The unit that matters is at the far end.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Unit seventeen's padlock is a different brand from the one in the photo on file. Changed recently. Why.
attribute.STEALTH: The corridors are built for privacy. One person uses it against another who thought they had the monopoly.
attribute.MEMORY: A different case, not long ago. The layout hasn't changed. The unit numbers memorised then are the ones needed now.

gender.male: The desk clerk hands over a unit key without questions. Men carrying boxes in a self-storage facility are the least suspicious thing in the world.
gender.female: The attendant is chatty. Three long-term renters' names and the rough contents of their units — all before the sign-in sheet is finished.

state.good: A clean single-storey or multi-storey building with straight roller shutter doors in uniform condition. The external perimeter fence and gate are intact and freshly painted.
state.normal: Several roller shutter doors show minor denting at the lower panels. The perimeter fence paint is fading and the gate hardware has surface rust.
state.bad: Multiple roller shutter doors are dented or misaligned, failing to close fully. The perimeter fence has bent sections and the gate drags against the ground surface, which has settled.

description.default: Stone and silence and the weight of things people carry through these doors that have nothing to do with faith.

time.morning: Morning service. The devout and the guilty occupy the same pews without knowing it. Sometimes they're the same person.
time.afternoon: The church opens to the community. Confession, quiet reflection, and the odd parishioner who lingers too long near the vestry.
time.evening: Evening prayer. The smaller crowd means fewer faces to read, and the ones who show up at this hour mean it.
time.night: Locked. A candle burning in the dark before the altar. Light in an empty church is either sacred or suspicious.

attribute.EMPATHY: The weight of every private grief that's passed through this room is considerable. It shows in the walls.
attribute.CHARISMA: The priest opens up. Something in the manner of the visitor says the conversation is worth having.
attribute.INTUITION: A place built for peace is currently not peaceful. Something brought someone here today that isn't on the schedule.

gender.male: The priest greets an unfamiliar man with careful warmth. The response in kind earns the second conversation.
gender.female: The congregation makes room immediately. A woman entering a church alone is assumed to have every reason in the world to be there.

state.good: A well-maintained stone or brick building with clean masonry and intact stained or plain window glazing. The entrance doors are solid and the roof line is even.
state.normal: The masonry shows surface weathering and some pointing has been replaced. The roof line is intact but several ridge tiles are slightly displaced, and the entrance door paint has begun to crack.
state.bad: Sections of the masonry have lost mortar and show open joints. The roof has patches of replacement slates in a different colour, and several window panes have cracked and been repaired with sealant. The entrance doors are warped and sit out of their frames.

description.default: A place of order in a city that has very little. The community here watches out for its own. A stranger is noticed before they notice anything.

time.morning: Fajr concluded, the mosque settling into its morning quiet. The men who pray before dawn are the ones worth knowing.
time.afternoon: Zuhr draws the midday congregation. Business of the community conducted in the margins of devotion.
time.evening: Maghrib as the light fades. The community gathering at the end of the working day. Loose ends get discussed here.
time.night: Isha, then silence. The mosque closes on the day with more dignity than the rest of the city manages.

attribute.EMPATHY: Respect at the threshold. The community reads sincerity in every movement. It either holds or it doesn't.
attribute.CHARISMA: Community elders acknowledge the visitor with the measured openness they extend to anyone who carries themselves without arrogance.
attribute.INTUITION: The welcome is genuine and the atmosphere is calm. The tension underneath it is neither.

gender.male: Shoes removed at the threshold. Silence kept. The space speaks first.
gender.female: Direction to the women's section, offered with courtesy. The conversation there is entirely different and significantly more useful.

state.good: A clean rendered or stone building with intact dome or minaret details and clear window glazing. The entrance courtyard is level and the exterior surfaces are freshly maintained.
state.normal: The exterior render shows minor crazing and the dome or minaret detail has surface weathering. The entrance courtyard has isolated cracks in its paving.
state.bad: The exterior render has delaminated in sections revealing bare masonry. Ornamental details on the dome or minaret show cracking and loss of material. The entrance courtyard paving is cracked and uneven in multiple areas.

description.default: History in every corner and a community that's survived enough to know how to read a stranger. The reading starts at the door.

time.morning: Morning minyan. The committed few who show up before the day starts. Short on excuses, long on information.
time.afternoon: Study sessions and committee business. The administrative life of the community running parallel to the spiritual one.
time.evening: Shabbat or weekday evening service. The week's events processed through the liturgy and the conversation afterward.
time.night: Locked. The Ner Tamid burns without end. The eternal light is the only honest thing still running at this hour.

attribute.EMPATHY: The community's depth of history is not academic here — it's lived. It meets a visitor at the door.
attribute.MEMORY: The archives in the back room go back further than any police record. The shammes knows where they are.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: A building that is simultaneously a house of worship, a community centre, and a library has more answers per square foot than most precincts.

gender.male: A kippah and a siddur placed in the hands of the visitor. Welcome extended, rhythm absorbed. The conversation comes after the service.
gender.female: The rebbetzin finds the visitor before orientation is complete. She's been watching the door. She always has been.

state.good: A clean stone or brick building with intact ornamental stonework and clear window glazing. The entrance doors and perimeter ironwork are freshly painted.
state.normal: The stonework shows surface weathering and some ornamental detail has minor losses. The entrance door paint is worn around the handle, and the perimeter ironwork has patches of surface rust.
state.bad: Stone facing has cracked and a section of ornamental detail has been lost. The entrance doors are swollen and difficult to operate, and the perimeter ironwork has significant corrosion with some sections replaced by plain flat bar.

description.default: People come here to be fixed or to die. Either way, they tell the truth. The right room is the one that holds it.

time.morning: Ward rounds and admissions, the machinery of care cranking into motion. The morning chaos is a current that carries anyone willing to move with it.
time.afternoon: Visiting hours. A tide of civilians moving through the building. The current flows in one direction and takes anyone in it along.
time.evening: Families saying goodnight. Corridors thinning. The staff starts to notice faces that don't belong.
time.night: Night staff and emergencies. Quieter, more alert, harder to bluff. The building is narrower after dark.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The chart rack holds more than the patient's charts were meant to contain. The information gap is structural. Nobody's closed it.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every person in this building is carrying something beyond their diagnosis. The weight is visible in the way they hold their shoulders, the way they won't meet anyone's eyes.
attribute.STAMINA: Hospitals never stop. The lights stay on, the corridors stay busy, the machinery of care runs through the night without pause.

gender.male: A man moving the ward with purpose and pace reads as someone who belongs there. Nobody stops him.
gender.female: A nurse stops to check on a woman in the corridor — genuine concern, no suspicion. One question asked and answered. Three others follow.

state.good: A clean institutional facade with even render and unscratched entrance glazing. The accessible drop-off area is clearly marked and the signage is fully illuminated.
state.normal: The render shows surface crazing at expansion joints. Several facade-mounted lights are dark and the accessible drop-off lane markings have begun to fade.
state.bad: Large sections of render have been patched with sealant that has since cracked. The glazed entrance screen has a cracked pane, and the facade-mounted signage is only partially illuminated.

description.default: A city inside a building. Corridors that go on forever and secrets that go on longer.

time.morning: The complex hums with the controlled chaos of morning handovers. A new shift is the best time to walk in unquestioned.
time.afternoon: Every wing active, every corridor navigable. A building this size needs a good reason to stop anyone.
time.evening: Visiting hours bring civilians. The cover they provide is dense and reliable.
time.night: Skeleton crew, long corridors, and something in wing C that doesn't belong in a hospital.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The unmanned records desk sits open at the end of the corridor. The supply chain manifest pinned beside it doesn't add up. The inefficiency is structural and nobody has fixed it.
attribute.EMPATHY: The weight of the place hits at the entrance and doesn't lift. The corridors carry it. The faces carry it harder.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A restricted corridor. The fluorescent hum runs overhead. Industrial cleaner and something underneath it — something the cleaner didn't reach.

gender.male: A man walking fast in a hospital reads as a consultant late for rounds. Nobody questions the pace.
gender.female: A woman walking fast in a hospital reads as a consultant late for rounds. It takes an extra thirty seconds to read that way, but it reads.

state.good: A substantial multi-wing facade of clean concrete and glass curtain wall. Every entrance is clearly signed and the approach roads are freshly painted.
state.normal: The concrete facade panels show weathering stains at the drainage points. Several sections of the glass curtain wall have isolated pane replacements, and approach road markings are worn.
state.bad: Exposed concrete has developed surface carbonation with rust blooms above several window lintels. Multiple panes in the curtain wall have been replaced with opaque infill panels, and the approach road surface is cracked and settled.

description.default: Not sick enough for the ER, too hurt to wait for a GP. The people here are at their rawest. The room holds that rawness like a bruise.

time.morning: Yesterday's bad decisions are today's overnight walk-ins. Every one of them has a story.
time.afternoon: Walk-ins stack up through the afternoon. The triage desk is stretched. Stretched people cut corners.
time.evening: Day's end injuries and weekend starts. The evening rush brings its own variety of truth.
time.night: Night-time cases are a different category entirely. The obvious cause is rarely the right one.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The injury doesn't match the explanation. Cleaner lies have been constructed with more preparation time than this.
attribute.STAMINA: The pace is relentless. The kind of place that strips away everything that isn't essential.
attribute.EMPATHY: Behind every ice pack and waiting room grimace is a story that started somewhere else. The presenting complaint rarely points to the origin.

gender.male: A man in the seat nearest the triage desk, holding his side. A pulled muscle. Nobody questions a pulled muscle.
gender.female: A woman moves through intake fast — the staff here move quickly on presenting patients. The access window is brief before anyone second-guesses it.

state.good: A clean modern facade with a large unscratched sign and clear entrance glazing. The accessible ramp and drop-off bay are freshly marked.
state.normal: The facade cladding is intact but shows minor surface scratches at trolley height. The entrance sign has one dark letter.
state.bad: The facade cladding has been patched in a mismatched colour where a section was replaced. The entrance sign is largely dark, and the accessible ramp has cracked along one edge.

description.default: Chrome and commission breath. Everyone in here is selling something. Some of them are selling cars.

time.morning: Salespeople reciting pitches before the floor opens. Pre-performance nerves. Good time to catch them off-script.
time.afternoon: Test drives and negotiation. Everybody's lying and everybody knows it.
time.evening: End of day, quota pressure, the desperation of people who need the numbers. They'll say anything.
time.night: Showroom lit up, no customers. The floor-to-ceiling glass holds the scene up to the street like an exhibit.

attribute.CHARISMA: The salesperson drops the canned pitch mid-sentence. Something in the room has shifted — the balance changed, the script abandoned. A real conversation starts.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Dealer invoice, holdback, regional incentive structure — the numbers behind every sticker price are visible to anyone who knows where to look. The salesperson stops performing the moment the margin is named.
attribute.INTUITION: The floor manager keeps looking at the lot. Not at the cars — at one specific space. And what it used to hold.

gender.male: They open with the high end. The conversation redirects in thirty seconds. The floor reads it as a man who knows what he wants.
gender.female: The salesperson underestimates the woman across the desk in the first ten seconds. By thirty, the hole is dug. The gap between assumption and reality is large enough to drive a case through.

state.good: Expanses of clear glazing reflect an even sky. The forecourt is clean and level, and the signage pylons are freshly painted with no visible rust.
state.normal: The glazing is intact but carries wiper arcs and minor crazing at the lower corners. A few forecourt light housings are cracked but functional.
state.bad: Several large panes in the glazed showroom wall have been replaced with opaque panels. The forecourt is cracked and uneven, and the signage pylon is rust-streaked from the fixing bolts down.

description.default: Grease and graft. This place fixes things that are broken. The broken things that matter most are never on the service menu.

time.morning: Engines turning over, diagnostics running. The day shift starts honest. Stays that way until the afternoon, usually.
time.afternoon: Bay full, every lift occupied. Good cover noise. The conversation running under the engine sounds carries further than it should.
time.evening: Last jobs being wrapped, overtime being argued. The day's secrets come out with the invoice.
time.night: The shutters are down. A light burns in the back office. That light doesn't belong to the books.

attribute.STRENGTH: The work here is honest and physical. The dialect of the floor is weight and leverage and things that don't argue back.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: A diagnostic code flagged on one vehicle cross-references cleanly with a second — one that wasn't brought in for repairs, and isn't on the board.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One car in the back bay wasn't brought in for repairs. The plates were changed in the last twelve hours.

gender.male: A man who speaks shop floor dialect gets treated like a regular. They relax. That's when the real answers come out.
gender.female: The mechanic starts over-explaining. It runs long and has a hole in it. The hole is big enough to drive a case through.

state.good: Smooth rendered walls and a wide clean workshop apron. The roller shutter doors are straight and fully operative, and the signage is clearly painted.
state.normal: Oil staining marks the apron at each bay opening. The roller shutter doors are functional but one has a dent. The exterior render shows crazing at the base.
state.bad: The render has peeled away in sections revealing bare block beneath. Workshop apron concrete has cracked into displaced sections, and one roller shutter door has jammed open.

description.default: Noise, sweat, and ten thousand people who aren't watching each other carefully enough. The gaps between their attention are wide.

time.morning: The morning rush is a river of blank faces. One of them is lying to someone. Probably more than one.
time.afternoon: The midday lull. Easier to pick out the ones who don't have anywhere to be.
time.evening: Tired workers, running feet, no eye contact. The evening crush is where people go invisible — at least in their own estimation.
time.night: The last trains scrape to a halt. Sparse crowd. Every face in this station is now accountable — nowhere left to thin into.

attribute.PERCEPTION: One figure stands stationary in a river of moving bodies. In a transit station, that's not waiting. That's watching.
attribute.AGILITY: Turnstiles, stairs, platforms — the station lays out a clean line through the crowd for anyone who reads the flow. Most people fight it. The space doesn't require that.
attribute.STAMINA: The noise and the press of bodies inside a transit station at peak hour would wear most people down to the nerve. The station doesn't offer a quiet corner. There isn't one.

gender.male: A man with his collar up and a purpose in his step moves through a transit station like a piece of it. Nobody gives him a second look. The station is full of him.
gender.female: A woman with her back to a pillar and her eyes on the exits is reading the room. In a crowded station, that kind of attention goes unremarked. Everyone's managing the same space differently.

state.good: Polished floor tiles carry the reflection of bright overhead panels. The timetable boards are sharp and unscratched, and the seating rows are freshly bolted.
state.normal: Scuff marks cover the floor tiles and one timetable board blinks intermittently. The seating rows are intact but worn smooth at the edges.
state.bad: Cracked tiles and missing grout sections pattern the floor. Several timetable boards are dark, and the metal seating frames are pocked with rust along every weld.

description.default: A narrow shopfront with a dense window display of camera housings, lock cylinders, and equipment cases. The interior is arranged so that every product is visible from the street.

time.morning: The steel security grille has been rolled up for the day. The window display is unchanged from yesterday, positioned to hold attention during the approach.
time.afternoon: The demonstration wall of camera housings is lit from inside the shop. The light makes the lens apertures visible from across the street.
time.evening: The grille is part-way down on its track. The window display lights are still running, picking out the product labels against the darkening glass.
time.night: The security grille is down and locked. A motion-activated light above the entrance comes on and goes off at irregular intervals.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The demonstration camera wall in the window shows a range of housing sizes, lens configurations, and mounting options. The coverage radius of each unit is printed on a small card beneath it.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The lock display case carries a range of cylinders from residential pin-tumbler to commercial multi-point systems. The technical specifications are printed on the shelf labels.

gender.male: The shopfront carries no signage above head height. The product in the window does the advertising — discrete equipment for people who do not want to advertise why they need it.
gender.female: The interior has a clear sight line from entrance to the counter at the back. The layout is deliberate — every approach to the cases is visible from the service position.

state.good: The shopfront glass is clean and the product display is neatly arranged behind it. The security grille operates smoothly and the entrance step is new and even.
state.normal: The shopfront glass carries faint smear marks at eye level and one item in the window display has been shifted out of its intended position. The grille has minor surface rust at one bracket.
state.bad: The shopfront glass has a crack running from the lower corner. The window display has gaps where stock has been removed without replacement. The security grille sticks on its track and the entrance step has a long surface crack.

description.default: Diesel and dead ends. The bus depot smells like every city that ever chewed someone up and spat them out.

time.morning: Routes being called, engines idling, the whole machine grinding to life. The morning churn produces cover — bodies moving in every direction, attention divided by the noise.
time.afternoon: The rhythm of the depot runs like clockwork — arrivals, departures, the same faces on the same rotations. Predictable enough to hide a deviation inside it.
time.evening: Last runs of the day. Drivers cutting corners, passengers in a hurry. The fatigue of a long shift leaves gaps in everyone's attention.
time.night: The yard crew moves through the dark like they've done it a thousand times. The routine is indistinguishable from its interruption, until it isn't.

attribute.STAMINA: Diesel fumes and engine noise fill the depot at all hours. The kind of environment that accumulates in the lungs and the ears. Not a space that accommodates comfort.
attribute.STRENGTH: Big machines, heavy work. The depot runs on physical labour — the loading, the coupling, the maintenance that happens at ground level in the grease and the dark.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Two buses carry the marks of recent movement — residual heat, fresh tyre marks on the apron. The logbook records neither departure. The gap between those two things is a story.

gender.male: A man who moves through the yard with the right nod reads as crew. In a depot this size, nobody's counting faces. The work uniform is posture.
gender.female: A woman in the yard draws eyes away from everything else for about thirty seconds. The depot crew's attention is a fixed resource — whatever it lands on, it leaves somewhere else uncovered.

state.good: The depot building presents clean rendered walls and freshly painted bay numbers. Fuel lines and drainage channels show no sign of leaks.
state.normal: Diesel staining marks the concrete apron around the bays. The wall render is intact but grey, and a few bay number signs have faded past easy reading.
state.bad: Oil has permanently darkened the cracked concrete of the apron. The rendered walls are mottled with damp stains and flaking paint, and several bay covers hang from single hinges.

parking_lot_small.default: A small surface parking lot. The painted lines are faded and the asphalt cracked.

state.good: A well-maintained parking lot with freshly painted lines.
state.normal: An ordinary surface parking lot. Cars come and go.
state.bad: A run-down lot full of potholes and faded markings.

parking_lot_large.default: A wide open parking lot stretching across the block.

state.good: A large, well-organised parking lot with clear signage.
state.normal: A busy parking lot where you search for a spot.
state.bad: A sprawling, neglected lot riddled with cracks and litter.

state.good: A clean, well-lit parking garage with smooth ramps.
state.normal: A functional parking garage. The fluorescent lights flicker occasionally.
state.bad: A gloomy parking garage with graffiti on every level.

state.good: A modern parking garage with electronic space indicators.
state.normal: A busy parking garage. You navigate its winding ramps with care.
state.bad: A dilapidated parking garage. Half the lights are out.

convenience_store.default: A convenience store with everything you need in a pinch.

time.morning: The morning crowd grabs coffee and newspapers.
time.evening: After-work shoppers browse the aisles.
time.night: A late-night run for snacks and essentials.

state.good: A bright, well-stocked convenience store with friendly staff.
state.normal: A typical convenience store. The shelves are reasonably full.
state.bad: A dingy store with sparse shelves and a sticky floor.

gas_station.default: A gas station with pumps and a small shop.

time.morning: Commuters queue at the pumps in the early morning rush.
time.evening: Drivers stop to refuel on their way home.
time.night: The gas station glows brightly against the dark street.

state.good: A modern gas station with working pumps and a clean shop.
state.normal: A standard gas station. The pumps are functional.
state.bad: An aging gas station. A few pumps are taped off with 'OUT OF ORDER' signs.

fast_food_restaurant.default: The smell of frying food hits you as you walk in.

time.morning: The breakfast crowd fills every table.
time.afternoon: Lunchtime rush — every counter is busy.
time.evening: The evening crowd is light but steady.
time.night: A handful of night owls linger over their meals.

state.good: A clean, modern fast food outlet with friendly service.
state.normal: A typical fast food restaurant. The line moves quickly.
state.bad: A tired fast food outlet. The tables are sticky and the floor unswept.

coffee_shop.default: The rich aroma of freshly ground coffee fills the air.

time.morning: The morning rush — a long line of caffeine-seekers.
time.afternoon: A relaxed afternoon crowd types away on laptops.
time.evening: A quieter evening crowd nurses their drinks.
time.night: Only a few late-nighters remain, nursing their cups.

state.good: A cosy, well-maintained coffee shop with excellent drinks.
state.normal: A pleasant coffee shop. The baristas are competent and friendly.
state.bad: A worn coffee shop. The espresso machine gurgles ominously.

small_retail_store.default: A small retail shop stocked with everyday goods.

state.good: A tidy little shop with well-arranged merchandise.
state.normal: A modest retail store. The selection is limited but adequate.
state.bad: A shabby store with half-empty shelves and faded price tags.

pharmacy.default: A pharmacy lined with medicines, vitamins, and health products.

time.morning: The pharmacy opens to a stream of early customers.
time.afternoon: A steady flow of customers picking up prescriptions.
time.evening: The evening rush — people collecting their prescriptions after work.

state.good: A clean, well-stocked pharmacy with knowledgeable staff.
state.normal: A standard pharmacy. The pharmacist is helpful.
state.bad: A cramped pharmacy with a long queue and sparse stock.

bank_branch.default: A bank branch with tellers behind a marble counter.

time.morning: The bank has just opened. A few early customers wait.
time.afternoon: The lunch crowd handles their banking needs.
time.evening: The branch is quieting down before closing time.

state.good: A well-appointed bank branch with fast, professional service.
state.normal: A standard bank branch. The queue moves at a steady pace.
state.bad: A run-down branch. The ATM is taped off and the wait is long.

laundromat.default: The hum of washing machines fills the warm, soapy air.

time.morning: A handful of early birds load their machines.
time.afternoon: People scroll their phones while waiting for their laundry.
time.evening: The evening crowd brings bags of washing.
time.night: A quiet night-time shift — just a few machines spinning.

state.good: A clean laundromat with modern machines and plenty of detergent.
state.normal: A typical laundromat. The machines work reliably enough.
state.bad: A grim laundromat. Half the machines are broken, the rest wobble noisily.

hair_salon.default: The snip of scissors and the hum of blow-dryers greet you.

gender.male: The barber spins a chair toward you with a confident nod.
gender.female: The stylist greets you warmly and gestures you to a seat.

state.good: A smart salon with stylish décor and skilled stylists.
state.normal: A pleasant enough salon. The stylists are friendly and competent.
state.bad: A faded salon. The mirrors are spotted and the chairs worn.

gym_fitness_center.default: The familiar smell of rubber mats and exertion fills the air.

time.morning: Early-morning regulars fill every machine.
time.afternoon: A moderate afternoon crowd works through their routines.
